<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/discourse/app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "mini_mime"
4: 
5: class UploadsController < ApplicationController
6:   include ExternalUploadHelpers
7:   include SecureUploadEndpointHelpers
8: 
9:   requires_login except: %i[show show_short _show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
10: 
11:   skip_before_action :preload_json,
12:                      :check_xhr,
13:                      :redirect_to_login_if_required,
14:                      :redirect_to_profile_if_required,
15:                      only: %i[show show_short _show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
16:   protect_from_forgery except: :show
17: 
18:   before_action :is_asset_path,
19:                 :apply_cdn_headers,
20:                 only: %i[show show_short _show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
21:   before_action :external_store_check, only: %i[_show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
22: 
23:   SECURE_REDIRECT_GRACE_SECONDS = 5
24: 
25:   def create
26:     # capture current user for block later on
27:     me = current_user
28:     RateLimiter.new(
29:       current_user,
30:       "uploads-per-minute",
31:       SiteSetting.max_uploads_per_minute,
32:       1.minute,
33:     ).performed!
34: 
35:     type =
36:       if params[:upload_type].presence
37:         params[:upload_type]
38:       elsif params[:type].presence
39:         Discourse.deprecate(
40:           "the :type param of `POST /uploads` is deprecated, use the :upload_type param instead",
41:           since: "3.4",
42:           drop_from: "3.5",
43:         )
44:         params[:type]
45:       else
46:         params.require(:upload_type)
47:       end
48:     # 50 characters ought to be enough for the upload type
49:     type = type.parameterize(separator: "_")[0..50]
50: 
51:     if type == "avatar" &&
52:          (
53:            SiteSetting.discourse_connect_overrides_avatar || SiteSetting.auth_overrides_avatar ||
54:              !me.in_any_groups?(SiteSetting.uploaded_avatars_allowed_groups_map)
55:          )
56:       return render json: failed_json, status: :unprocessable_entity
57:     end
58: 
59:     url = params[:url]
60:     file = params[:file] || params[:files]&.first
61:     pasted = params[:pasted] == "true"
62:     for_private_message = params[:for_private_message] == "true"
63:     for_site_setting = params[:for_site_setting] == "true"
64:     site_setting_name = for_site_setting ? params[:site_setting_name] : nil
65:     is_api = is_api?
66:     retain_hours = params[:retain_hours].to_i
67: 
68:     # note, atm hijack is processed in its own context and has not access to controller
69:     # longer term we may change this
70:     hijack do
71:       begin
72:         info =
73:           UploadsController.create_upload(
74:             current_user: me,
75:             file:,
76:             url:,
77:             type:,
78:             for_private_message:,
79:             for_site_setting:,
80:             site_setting_name:,
81:             pasted:,
82:             is_api:,
83:             retain_hours:,
84:           )
85:       rescue => e
86:         render json: failed_json.merge(message: e.message&.split("\n")&.first),
87:                status: :unprocessable_entity
88:       else
89:         render json: UploadsController.serialize_upload(info), status: Upload === info ? 200 : 422
90:       end
91:     end
92:   end
93: 
94:   def lookup_urls
95:     params.permit(short_urls: [])
96:     uploads = []
97: 
98:     if (params[:short_urls] && params[:short_urls].length > 0)
99:       PrettyText::Helpers
100:         .lookup_upload_urls(params[:short_urls])
101:         .each do |short_url, paths|
102:           uploads << { short_url: short_url, url: paths[:url], short_path: paths[:short_path] }
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106:     render json: uploads.to_json
107:   end
108: 
109:   def show
110:     # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
111:     return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
112: 
113:     return render_404 if !RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.has_db?(params[:site])
114: 
115:     begin
116:       request_site_current_user =
117:         request.env.delete(Auth::DefaultCurrentUserProvider::CURRENT_USER_KEY)
118: 
119:       RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.with_connection(params[:site]) do |db|
120:         begin
121:           # current_user here refers to the user for the site that we are operating on
122:           # using with_connection. If DB for the target site matches the current site
123:           # for the request, then current_user will be the same as the request_site_current_user
124:           return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
125: 
126:           upload =
127:             Upload.find_by(sha1: params[:sha]) ||
128:               Upload.find_by(id: params[:id], url: request.env["PATH_INFO"])
129: 
130:           if upload.present?
131:             if !Discourse.store.internal?
132:               local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
133:               return render_404 unless local_store.has_been_uploaded?(upload.url)
134:             end
135: 
136:             send_file_local_upload(upload)
137:           else
138:             render_404
139:           end
140:         end
141:       end
142:     ensure
143:       request.env[Auth::DefaultCurrentUserProvider::CURRENT_USER_KEY] = request_site_current_user
144:     end
145:   end
146: 
147:   def show_short
148:     # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
149:     return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
150: 
151:     return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
152: 
153:     sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(params[:base62])
154: 
155:     if upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
156:       return handle_secure_upload_request(upload) if upload.secure? && SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
157: 
158:       if Discourse.store.internal?
159:         send_file_local_upload(upload)
160:       else
161:         redirect_to Discourse.store.url_for(upload, force_download: force_download?),
162:                     allow_other_host: true
163:       end
164:     else
165:       render_404
166:     end
167:   end
168: 
169:   # Kept to avoid rebaking old posts with /show-secure-uploads/ in their
170:   # contents, this will ensure the uploads in these posts continue to
171:   # work in future.
172:   def _show_secure_deprecated
173:     show_secure
174:   end
175: 
176:   def show_secure
177:     # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
178:     return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
179: 
180:     path_with_ext =
181:       params[:extension].nil? ? params[:path] : "#{params[:path]}.#{params[:extension]}"
182:     upload = upload_from_path_and_extension(path_with_ext)
183: 
184:     return render_404 if upload.blank?
185: 
186:     return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
187:     return handle_secure_upload_request(upload, path_with_ext) if SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
188: 
189:     # we don't want to 404 here if secure uploads gets disabled
190:     # because all posts with secure uploads will show broken media
191:     # until rebaked, which could take some time
192:     #
193:     # if the upload is still secure, that means the ACL is probably still
194:     # private, so we don't want to go to the CDN url just yet otherwise we
195:     # will get a 403. if the upload is not secure we assume the ACL is public
196:     signed_secure_url =
197:       Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(
198:         path_with_ext,
199:         filename: upload.original_filename,
200:         include_content_disposition: true,
201:       )
202:     redirect_to upload.secure? ? signed_secure_url : Discourse.store.cdn_url(upload.url),
203:                 allow_other_host: true
204:   end
205: 
206:   def handle_secure_upload_request(upload, path_with_ext = nil)
207:     check_secure_upload_permission(upload)
208: 
209:     # defaults to public: false, so only cached by the client browser
210:     cache_seconds =
211:       SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds - SECURE_REDIRECT_GRACE_SECONDS
212:     expires_in cache_seconds.seconds
213: 
214:     # url_for figures out the full URL, handling multisite DBs,
215:     # and will return a presigned URL for the upload
216:     if path_with_ext.blank?
217:       return(
218:         redirect_to Discourse.store.url_for(upload, force_download: force_download?),
219:                     allow_other_host: true
220:       )
221:     end
222: 
223:     redirect_to Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(
224:                   path_with_ext,
225:                   expires_in: SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds,
226:                   force_download: force_download?,
227:                   filename: upload.original_filename,
228:                   include_content_disposition: true,
229:                 ),
230:                 allow_other_host: true
231:   end
232: 
233:   def metadata
234:     params.require(:url)
235:     upload = Upload.get_from_url(params[:url])
236:     raise Discourse::NotFound unless upload
237: 
238:     render json: {
239:              original_filename: upload.original_filename,
240:              width: upload.width,
241:              height: upload.height,
242:              human_filesize: upload.human_filesize,
243:            }
244:   end
245: 
246:   protected
247: 
248:   def validate_before_create_multipart(file_name:, file_size:, upload_type:)
249:     validate_file_size(file_name: file_name, file_size: file_size)
250:   end
251: 
252:   def validate_before_create_direct_upload(file_name:, file_size:, upload_type:)
253:     validate_file_size(file_name: file_name, file_size: file_size)
254:   end
255: 
256:   def validate_file_size(file_name:, file_size:)
257:     raise ExternalUploadValidationError.new(I18n.t("upload.size_zero_failure")) if file_size.zero?
258: 
259:     if attachment_too_big?(file_name, file_size)
260:       raise ExternalUploadValidationError.new(
261:               I18n.t(
262:                 "upload.attachments.too_large_humanized",
263:                 max_size:
264:                   ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(
265:                     UploadsController.max_attachment_size_for_user(current_user).kilobytes,
266:                   ),
267:               ),
268:             )
269:     end
270: 
271:     if image_too_big?(file_name, file_size)
272:       raise ExternalUploadValidationError.new(
273:               I18n.t(
274:                 "upload.images.too_large_humanized",
275:                 max_size:
276:                   ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(
277:                     SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes,
278:                   ),
279:               ),
280:             )
281:     end
282:   end
283: 
284:   def force_download?
285:     params[:dl] == "1"
286:   end
287: 
288:   def xhr_not_allowed
289:     raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new("XHR not allowed")
290:   end
291: 
292:   def self.serialize_upload(data)
293:     # as_json.as_json is not a typo... as_json in AM serializer returns keys as symbols, we need them
294:     # as strings here
295:     serialized = UploadSerializer.new(data, root: nil).as_json.as_json if Upload === data
296:     serialized ||= (data || {}).as_json
297:   end
298: 
299:   def self.create_upload(
300:     current_user:,
301:     file:,
302:     url:,
303:     type:,
304:     for_private_message:,
305:     for_site_setting:,
306:     site_setting_name: nil,
307:     pasted:,
308:     is_api:,
309:     retain_hours:
310:   )
311:     if file.nil?
312:       if url.present? && is_api
313:         maximum_upload_size = [
314:           SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
315:           UploadsController.max_attachment_size_for_user(current_user),
316:         ].max.kilobytes
317:         tempfile =
318:           begin
319:             FileHelper.download(
320:               url,
321:               follow_redirect: true,
322:               max_file_size: maximum_upload_size,
323:               tmp_file_name: "discourse-upload-#{type}",
324:             )
325:           rescue StandardError
326:             nil
327:           end
328:         filename = File.basename(URI.parse(url).path)
329:       end
330:     else
331:       tempfile = file.tempfile
332:       filename = file.original_filename
333:     end
334: 
335:     return { errors: [I18n.t("upload.file_missing")] } if tempfile.nil?
336: 
337:     opts = { type:, for_private_message:, for_site_setting:, site_setting_name:, pasted: }
338: 
339:     upload = UploadCreator.new(tempfile, filename, opts).create_for(current_user.id)
340: 
341:     if upload.errors.empty? && current_user.admin?
342:       upload.update_columns(retain_hours: retain_hours) if retain_hours > 0
343:     end
344: 
345:     upload.errors.empty? ? upload : { errors: upload.errors.to_hash.values.flatten }
346:   ensure
347:     tempfile&.close!
348:   end
349: 
350:   private
351: 
352:   def self.max_attachment_size_for_user(user)
353:     if user.id == Discourse::SYSTEM_USER_ID && !SiteSetting.system_user_max_attachment_size_kb.zero?
354:       SiteSetting.system_user_max_attachment_size_kb
355:     else
356:       SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb
357:     end
358:   end
359: 
360:   # We can preemptively check size for attachments, but not for (most) images
361:   # as they may be further reduced in size by UploadCreator (at this point
362:   # they may have already been reduced in size by preprocessors)
363:   def attachment_too_big?(file_name, file_size)
364:     !FileHelper.is_supported_image?(file_name) &&
365:       file_size >= UploadsController.max_attachment_size_for_user(current_user).kilobytes
366:   end
367: 
368:   # Gifs are not resized on the client and not reduced in size by UploadCreator
369:   def image_too_big?(file_name, file_size)
370:     FileHelper.is_supported_image?(file_name) && File.extname(file_name) == ".gif" &&
371:       file_size >= SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes
372:   end
373: 
374:   def send_file_local_upload(upload)
375:     opts = {
376:       filename: upload.original_filename,
377:       content_type: MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(upload.original_filename)&.content_type,
378:     }
379: 
380:     if !FileHelper.is_inline_safe?(upload.original_filename)
381:       opts[:disposition] = "attachment"
382:     elsif params[:inline]
383:       opts[:disposition] = "inline"
384:     end
385: 
386:     response.headers["Content-Security-Policy"] = "sandbox;"
387: 
388:     file_path = Discourse.store.path_for(upload)
389:     return render_404 unless file_path && File.exist?(file_path)
390: 
391:     send_file(file_path, opts)
392:   end
393: 
394:   def create_direct_multipart_upload
395:     begin
396:       yield
397:     rescue Aws::S3::Errors::ServiceError => err
398:       message =
399:         debug_upload_error(
400:           err,
401:           I18n.t("upload.create_multipart_failure", additional_detail: err.message),
402:         )
403:       raise ExternalUploadHelpers::ExternalUploadValidationError.new(message)
404:     end
405:   end
406: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module SecureUploadEndpointHelpers
4:   include ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   def upload_from_path_and_extension(path_with_ext)
7:     sha1 = File.basename(path_with_ext, File.extname(path_with_ext))
8:     # this takes care of optimized image requests
9:     sha1 = sha1.partition("_").first if sha1.include?("_")
10:     Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
11:   end
12: 
13:   def upload_from_full_url(url)
14:     Upload.find_by(sha1: Upload.sha1_from_long_url(url))
15:   end
16: 
17:   def check_secure_upload_permission(upload)
18:     if upload.access_control_post_id.present?
19:       raise Discourse::InvalidAccess if current_user.nil? && SiteSetting.login_required
20:       raise Discourse::InvalidAccess if !guardian.can_see?(upload.access_control_post)
21:     else
22:       raise Discourse::NotFound if current_user.nil?
23:     end
24:   end
25: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # Extends controllers with the methods required to do direct
4: # external uploads.
5: module ExternalUploadHelpers
6:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
7: 
8:   class ExternalUploadValidationError < StandardError
9:   end
10: 
11:   included do
12:     before_action :external_store_check,
13:                   only: %i[
14:                     generate_presigned_put
15:                     complete_external_upload
16:                     create_multipart
17:                     batch_presign_multipart_parts
18:                     abort_multipart
19:                     complete_multipart
20:                   ]
21:     before_action :direct_s3_uploads_check,
22:                   only: %i[
23:                     generate_presigned_put
24:                     complete_external_upload
25:                     create_multipart
26:                     batch_presign_multipart_parts
27:                     abort_multipart
28:                     complete_multipart
29:                   ]
30:     before_action :can_upload_external?, only: %i[create_multipart generate_presigned_put]
31:   end
32: 
33:   def generate_presigned_put
34:     RateLimiter.new(
35:       current_user,
36:       "generate-presigned-put-upload-stub",
37:       SiteSetting.max_presigned_put_per_minute,
38:       1.minute,
39:     ).performed!
40: 
41:     file_name = params.require(:file_name)
42:     file_size = params.require(:file_size).to_i
43:     type = params.require(:type)
44: 
45:     begin
46:       validate_before_create_direct_upload(
47:         file_name: file_name,
48:         file_size: file_size,
49:         upload_type: type,
50:       )
51:     rescue ExternalUploadValidationError => err
52:       return render_json_error(err.message, status: 422)
53:     end
54: 
55:     external_upload_data =
56:       ExternalUploadManager.create_direct_upload(
57:         current_user: current_user,
58:         file_name: file_name,
59:         file_size: file_size,
60:         upload_type: type,
61:         metadata: parse_allowed_metadata(params[:metadata]),
62:       )
63: 
64:     render json: external_upload_data
65:   end
66: 
67:   def complete_external_upload
68:     unique_identifier = params.require(:unique_identifier)
69:     external_upload_stub =
70:       ExternalUploadStub.find_by(unique_identifier: unique_identifier, created_by: current_user)
71:     return render_404 if external_upload_stub.blank?
72: 
73:     complete_external_upload_via_manager(external_upload_stub)
74:   end
75: 
76:   def create_multipart
77:     RateLimiter.new(
78:       current_user,
79:       "create-multipart-upload",
80:       SiteSetting.max_create_multipart_per_minute,
81:       1.minute,
82:     ).performed!
83: 
84:     file_name = params.require(:file_name)
85:     file_size = params.require(:file_size).to_i
86:     upload_type = params.require(:upload_type)
87: 
88:     begin
89:       validate_before_create_multipart(
90:         file_name: file_name,
91:         file_size: file_size,
92:         upload_type: upload_type,
93:       )
94:     rescue ExternalUploadValidationError => err
95:       return render_json_error(err.message, status: 422)
96:     end
97: 
98:     begin
99:       external_upload_data =
100:         create_direct_multipart_upload do
101:           ExternalUploadManager.create_direct_multipart_upload(
102:             current_user: current_user,
103:             file_name: file_name,
104:             file_size: file_size,
105:             upload_type: upload_type,
106:             metadata: parse_allowed_metadata(params[:metadata]),
107:           )
108:         end
109:     rescue ExternalUploadHelpers::ExternalUploadValidationError => err
110:       return render_json_error(err.message, status: 422)
111:     end
112: 
113:     render json: external_upload_data
114:   end
115: 
116:   def batch_presign_multipart_parts
117:     part_numbers = params.require(:part_numbers)
118:     unique_identifier = params.require(:unique_identifier)
119: 
120:     ##
121:     # NOTE: This is configurable by hidden site setting because this really is heavily
122:     # dependent on upload speed. We request 5-10 URLs at a time with this endpoint; for
123:     # a 1.5GB upload with 5mb parts this could mean 60 requests to the server to get all
124:     # the part URLs. If the user's upload speed is super fast they may request all 60
125:     # batches in a minute, if it is slow they may request 5 batches in a minute.
126:     RateLimiter.new(
127:       current_user,
128:       "batch-presign",
129:       SiteSetting.max_batch_presign_multipart_per_minute,
130:       1.minute,
131:     ).performed!
132: 
133:     part_numbers = part_numbers.map { |part_number| validate_part_number(part_number) }
134: 
135:     external_upload_stub =
136:       ExternalUploadStub.find_by(unique_identifier: unique_identifier, created_by: current_user)
137:     return render_404 if external_upload_stub.blank?
138: 
139:     message = check_multipart_upload_exists(external_upload_stub)
140:     return render_404(message) if message.present?
141: 
142:     store = multipart_store(external_upload_stub.upload_type)
143: 
144:     presigned_urls = {}
145:     part_numbers.each do |part_number|
146:       presigned_urls[part_number] = store.presign_multipart_part(
147:         upload_id: external_upload_stub.external_upload_identifier,
148:         key: external_upload_stub.key,
149:         part_number: part_number,
150:       )
151:     end
152: 
153:     render json: { presigned_urls: presigned_urls }
154:   end
155: 
156:   def check_multipart_upload_exists(external_upload_stub)
157:     store = multipart_store(external_upload_stub.upload_type)
158:     begin
159:       store.list_multipart_parts(
160:         upload_id: external_upload_stub.external_upload_identifier,
161:         key: external_upload_stub.key,
162:         max_parts: 1,
163:       )
164:     rescue Aws::S3::Errors::NoSuchUpload => err
165:       return(
166:         debug_upload_error(
167:           err,
168:           I18n.t(
169:             "upload.external_upload_not_found",
170:             additional_detail: "path: #{external_upload_stub.key}",
171:           ),
172:         )
173:       )
174:     end
175: 
176:     # Intentional to indicate that there is no error, if there is a message
177:     # then there is an error.
178:     nil
179:   end
180: 
181:   def abort_multipart
182:     external_upload_identifier = params.require(:external_upload_identifier)
183:     external_upload_stub =
184:       ExternalUploadStub.find_by(external_upload_identifier: external_upload_identifier)
185: 
186:     # The stub could have already been deleted by an earlier error via
187:     # ExternalUploadManager, so we consider this a great success if the
188:     # stub is already gone.
189:     return render json: success_json if external_upload_stub.blank?
190: 
191:     return render_404 if external_upload_stub.created_by_id != current_user.id
192:     store = multipart_store(external_upload_stub.upload_type)
193: 
194:     begin
195:       store.abort_multipart(
196:         upload_id: external_upload_stub.external_upload_identifier,
197:         key: external_upload_stub.key,
198:       )
199:     rescue Aws::S3::Errors::ServiceError => err
200:       return(
201:         render_json_error(
202:           debug_upload_error(
203:             err,
204:             I18n.t(
205:               "upload.abort_multipart_failure",
206:               additional_detail: "external upload stub id: #{external_upload_stub.id}",
207:             ),
208:           ),
209:           status: 422,
210:         )
211:       )
212:     end
213: 
214:     external_upload_stub.destroy!
215: 
216:     render json: success_json
217:   end
218: 
219:   def complete_multipart
220:     unique_identifier = params.require(:unique_identifier)
221:     parts = params.require(:parts)
222: 
223:     RateLimiter.new(
224:       current_user,
225:       "complete-multipart-upload",
226:       SiteSetting.max_complete_multipart_per_minute,
227:       1.minute,
228:     ).performed!
229: 
230:     external_upload_stub =
231:       ExternalUploadStub.find_by(unique_identifier: unique_identifier, created_by: current_user)
232:     return render_404 if external_upload_stub.blank?
233: 
234:     message = check_multipart_upload_exists(external_upload_stub)
235:     return render_404(message) if message.present?
236: 
237:     store = multipart_store(external_upload_stub.upload_type)
238:     parts =
239:       parts
240:         .map do |part|
241:           part_number = part[:part_number]
242:           etag = part[:etag]
243:           part_number = validate_part_number(part_number)
244: 
245:           if etag.blank?
246:             raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(
247:                     "All parts must have an etag and a valid part number",
248:                   )
249:           end
250: 
251:           # this is done so it's an array of hashes rather than an array of
252:           # ActionController::Parameters
253:           { part_number: part_number, etag: etag }
254:         end
255:         .sort_by { |part| part[:part_number] }
256: 
257:     begin
258:       store.complete_multipart(
259:         upload_id: external_upload_stub.external_upload_identifier,
260:         key: external_upload_stub.key,
261:         parts: parts,
262:       )
263:     rescue Aws::S3::Errors::ServiceError => err
264:       return(
265:         render_json_error(
266:           debug_upload_error(
267:             err,
268:             I18n.t(
269:               "upload.complete_multipart_failure",
270:               additional_detail: "external upload stub id: #{external_upload_stub.id}",
271:             ),
272:           ),
273:           status: 422,
274:         )
275:       )
276:     end
277: 
278:     complete_external_upload_via_manager(external_upload_stub)
279:   end
280: 
281:   private
282: 
283:   def complete_external_upload_via_manager(external_upload_stub)
284:     for_site_setting = params[:for_site_setting]&.to_s == "true"
285:     opts = {
286:       for_private_message: params[:for_private_message]&.to_s == "true",
287:       for_site_setting:,
288:       site_setting_name: for_site_setting ? params[:site_setting_name] : nil,
289:       pasted: params[:pasted]&.to_s == "true",
290:     }
291: 
292:     external_upload_manager = ExternalUploadManager.new(external_upload_stub, opts)
293:     hijack do
294:       begin
295:         upload = external_upload_manager.transform!
296: 
297:         if upload.errors.empty?
298:           response_serialized = self.class.serialize_upload(upload)
299:           external_upload_stub.destroy!
300:           render json: response_serialized, status: :ok
301:         else
302:           render_json_error(upload.errors.to_hash.values.flatten, status: 422)
303:         end
304:       rescue ExternalUploadManager::SizeMismatchError => err
305:         render_json_error(
306:           debug_upload_error(
307:             err,
308:             I18n.t("upload.size_mismatch_failure", additional_detail: err.message),
309:           ),
310:           status: 422,
311:         )
312:       rescue ExternalUploadManager::ChecksumMismatchError => err
313:         render_json_error(
314:           debug_upload_error(
315:             err,
316:             I18n.t("upload.checksum_mismatch_failure", additional_detail: err.message),
317:           ),
318:           status: 422,
319:         )
320:       rescue ExternalUploadManager::CannotPromoteError => err
321:         render_json_error(
322:           debug_upload_error(
323:             err,
324:             I18n.t("upload.cannot_promote_failure", additional_detail: err.message),
325:           ),
326:           status: 422,
327:         )
328:       rescue ExternalUploadManager::DownloadFailedError, Aws::S3::Errors::NotFound => err
329:         render_json_error(
330:           debug_upload_error(
331:             err,
332:             I18n.t("upload.download_failure", additional_detail: err.message),
333:           ),
334:           status: 422,
335:         )
336:       rescue => err
337:         Rails.logger.error(
338:           "Complete external upload failed unexpectedly for user #{current_user.id}: #{err.class} #{err.message}\n#{err.backtrace.join("\n")}",
339:         )
340: 
341:         render_json_error(I18n.t("upload.failed"), status: 422)
342:       end
343:     end
344:   end
345: 
346:   def validate_before_create_direct_upload(file_name:, file_size:, upload_type:)
347:     # noop, should be overridden
348:   end
349: 
350:   def validate_before_create_multipart(file_name:, file_size:, upload_type:)
351:     # noop, should be overridden
352:   end
353: 
354:   def validate_part_number(part_number)
355:     part_number = part_number.to_i
356:     if !part_number.between?(1, 10_000)
357:       raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new("Each part number should be between 1 and 10000")
358:     end
359:     part_number
360:   end
361: 
362:   def debug_upload_error(err, friendly_message)
363:     return I18n.t("upload.failed") if !SiteSetting.enable_upload_debug_mode
364:     Discourse.warn_exception(err, message: "[ExternalUploadError] #{friendly_message}")
365: 
366:     if Rails.env.local? || is_api?
367:       friendly_message
368:     else
369:       I18n.t("upload.failed")
370:     end
371:   end
372: 
373:   def multipart_store(upload_type)
374:     ExternalUploadManager.store_for_upload_type(upload_type)
375:   end
376: 
377:   def external_store_check
378:     render_404 if !Discourse.store.external?
379:   end
380: 
381:   def direct_s3_uploads_check
382:     render_404 if !SiteSetting.enable_direct_s3_uploads
383:   end
384: 
385:   def can_upload_external?
386:     raise Discourse::InvalidAccess if !guardian.can_upload_external?
387:   end
388: 
389:   # don't want people posting arbitrary S3 metadata so we just take the
390:   # one we need. all of these will be converted to x-amz-meta- metadata
391:   # fields in S3 so it's best to use dashes in the names for consistency
392:   #
393:   # this metadata is baked into the presigned url and is not altered when
394:   # sending the PUT from the clientside to the presigned url
395:   def parse_allowed_metadata(metadata)
396:     return if metadata.blank?
397:     metadata.permit("sha1-checksum").to_h
398:   end
399: 
400:   def render_404(message = nil)
401:     if message
402:       render_json_error(message, status: 404)
403:     else
404:       raise Discourse::NotFound
405:     end
406:   end
407: end

(End of file - total 407 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module BackupRestore
4:   UploadsRestoreError = Class.new(RuntimeError)
5: 
6:   class UploadsRestorer
7:     delegate :log, to: :@logger, private: true
8: 
9:     S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX = /\.s3(?:\.dualstack\.[a-z0-9\-]+?|[.\-][a-z0-9\-]+?)?\.amazonaws\.com/
10: 
11:     def self.s3_regex_string(s3_base_url)
12:       clean_url = s3_base_url.sub(S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX, ".s3.amazonaws.com")
13: 
14:       regex_string =
15:         clean_url
16:           .split(".s3.amazonaws.com")
17:           .map { |s| Regexp.escape(s) }
18:           .insert(1, S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX.source)
19:           .join("")
20: 
21:       [regex_string, clean_url]
22:     end
23: 
24:     def initialize(logger)
25:       @logger = logger
26:     end
27: 
28:     def restore(tmp_directory)
29:       upload_directories =
30:         Dir
31:           .glob(File.join(tmp_directory, "uploads", "*"))
32:           .reject { |path| File.basename(path).start_with?("PaxHeaders") }
33: 
34:       if upload_directories.count > 1
35:         raise UploadsRestoreError.new(
36:                 "Could not find uploads, because the uploads " \
37:                   "directory contains multiple folders.",
38:               )
39:       end
40: 
41:       @tmp_uploads_path = upload_directories.first
42:       return if @tmp_uploads_path.blank?
43: 
44:       @previous_db_name = BackupMetadata.value_for("db_name") || File.basename(@tmp_uploads_path)
45:       @current_db_name = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
46:       backup_contains_optimized_images = File.exist?(File.join(@tmp_uploads_path, "optimized"))
47: 
48:       remap_uploads
49:       restore_uploads
50: 
51:       generate_optimized_images unless backup_contains_optimized_images
52:       rebake_posts_with_uploads
53:     end
54: 
55:     protected
56: 
57:     def restore_uploads
58:       store = Discourse.store
59: 
60:       if !store.respond_to?(:copy_from)
61:         # a FileStore implementation from a plugin might not support this method, so raise a helpful error
62:         store_name = Discourse.store.class.name
63:         raise UploadsRestoreError.new(
64:                 "The current file store (#{store_name}) does not support restoring uploads.",
65:               )
66:       end
67: 
68:       log "Restoring uploads, this may take a while..."
69:       store.copy_from(@tmp_uploads_path)
70:     end
71: 
72:     # Remaps upload URLs depending on old and new configuration.
73:     # URLs of uploads differ a little bit between local uploads and uploads stored on S3.
74:     # Multisites are another reason why URLs can be different.
75:     #
76:     # Examples:
77:     #   * regular site, local storage
78:     #     /uploads/default/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
79:     #
80:     #   * regular site, S3
81:     #     //bucket-name.s3.dualstack.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
82:     #
83:     #   * multisite, local storage
84:     #     /uploads/<site-name>/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
85:     #
86:     #   * multisite, S3
87:     #     //bucket-name.s3.dualstack.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/uploads/<site-name>/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
88:     def remap_uploads
89:       log "Remapping uploads..."
90: 
91:       was_multisite = BackupMetadata.value_for("multisite") == "t"
92:       upload_path = "/#{Discourse.store.upload_path}/"
93:       uploads_folder = was_multisite ? "/" : upload_path
94: 
95:       if (old_base_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("base_url")) && old_base_url != Discourse.base_url
96:         remap(old_base_url, Discourse.base_url)
97:       end
98: 
99:       current_s3_base_url =
100:         SiteSetting::Upload.enable_s3_uploads ? SiteSetting::Upload.s3_base_url : nil
101:       if (old_s3_base_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("s3_base_url")) &&
102:            old_s3_base_url != current_s3_base_url
103:         remap_s3("#{old_s3_base_url}/", uploads_folder)
104:       end
105: 
106:       current_s3_cdn_url =
107:         SiteSetting::Upload.enable_s3_uploads ? SiteSetting::Upload.s3_cdn_url : nil
108:       if (old_s3_cdn_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("s3_cdn_url")) &&
109:            old_s3_cdn_url != current_s3_cdn_url
110:         base_url = current_s3_cdn_url || Discourse.base_url
111:         remap("#{old_s3_cdn_url}/", UrlHelper.schemaless("#{base_url}#{uploads_folder}"))
112: 
113:         old_host = URI.parse(old_s3_cdn_url).host
114:         new_host = URI.parse(base_url).host
115:         remap(old_host, new_host) if old_host != new_host
116:       end
117: 
118:       if (old_cdn_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("cdn_url")) && old_cdn_url != Discourse.asset_host
119:         base_url = Discourse.asset_host || Discourse.base_url
120:         remap("#{old_cdn_url}/", UrlHelper.schemaless("#{base_url}/"))
121: 
122:         old_host = URI.parse(old_cdn_url).host
123:         new_host = URI.parse(base_url).host
124:         remap(old_host, new_host) if old_host != new_host
125:       end
126: 
127:       remap("/uploads/#{@previous_db_name}/", upload_path) if @previous_db_name != @current_db_name
128:     rescue => ex
129:       log "Something went wrong while remapping uploads.", ex
130:     end
131: 
132:     def remap(from, to)
133:       log "Remapping '#{from}' to '#{to}'"
134:       DbHelper.remap(from, to, verbose: true, excluded_tables: ["backup_metadata"])
135:     end
136: 
137:     def remap_s3(old_s3_base_url, uploads_folder)
138:       if old_s3_base_url.include?("amazonaws.com")
139:         from_regex, from_clean_url = self.class.s3_regex_string(old_s3_base_url)
140:         log "Remapping with regex from '#{from_clean_url}' to '#{uploads_folder}'"
141:         DbHelper.regexp_replace(
142:           from_regex,
143:           uploads_folder,
144:           verbose: true,
145:           excluded_tables: ["backup_metadata"],
146:         )
147:       else
148:         remap(old_s3_base_url, uploads_folder)
149:       end
150:     end
151: 
152:     def generate_optimized_images
153:       log "Optimizing site icons..."
154:       DB.exec("TRUNCATE TABLE optimized_images")
155:       SiteIconManager.ensure_optimized!
156: 
157:       User
158:         .where.not(uploaded_avatar_id: nil)
159:         .find_each do |user|
160:           Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, upload_id: user.uploaded_avatar_id)
161:         end
162:     end
163: 
164:     def rebake_posts_with_uploads
165:       log "Posts will be rebaked by a background job in sidekiq. You will see missing images until that has completed."
166:       log 'You can expedite the process by manually running "rake posts:rebake_uncooked_posts"'
167: 
168:       DB.exec(<<~SQL)
169:         UPDATE posts
170:         SET baked_version = NULL
171:         WHERE id IN (SELECT target_id FROM upload_references WHERE target_type = 'Post')
172:       SQL
173:     end
174:   end
175: end

3: class ThemeSettingsManager::Upload < ThemeSettingsManager

6:   end

10:     return if upload_id.blank?
11:

13:   end

17:       if new_value == default
18:         new_value = default_upload_id
19:       else

7:   class BaseStore
8:     UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX = %r{/(original/\d+X/.*)}
9:     OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX = %r{/(optimized/\d+X/.*)}
10:     TEMPORARY_UPLOAD_PREFIX = "temp/"
11: 
12:     def store_upload(file, upload, content_type = nil)
13:       upload.url = nil
14:       path = get_path_for_upload(upload)
15:       store_file(file, path)

40:     def upload_path
41:       path = File.join("uploads", RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db)
42:       return path if !Rails.env.test?
43:       File.join(path, "test_#{ENV["TEST_ENV_NUMBER"].presence || "0"}")

46:     def self.temporary_upload_path(file_name, folder_prefix: "")
47:       # We don't want to use the original file name as it can contain special
48:       # characters, which can interfere with external providers operations and
49:       # introduce other unexpected behaviour.
50:       file_name_random = "#{SecureRandom.hex}#{File.extname(file_name)}"
51:       File.join(TEMPORARY_UPLOAD_PREFIX, folder_prefix, SecureRandom.hex, file_name_random)
52:     end

54:     def has_been_uploaded?(url)
55:       not_implemented
56:     end

58:     def download_url(upload)
59:       not_implemented
60:     end

63:       not_implemented
64:     end

67:       not_implemented
68:     end
69: 
70:     def relative_base_url
71:       not_implemented
72:     end

75:       not_implemented

87:       not_implemented

91:       not_implemented

94:     def download(*, **)
95:       perform_download(*, **)
96:     rescue StandardError
97:       nil
98:     end
99: 
100:     def download_safe(*, **)
101:       Discourse.deprecate(
102:         "`FileStore::BaseStore#download_safe` is deprecated. Use `#download` instead.",
103:         since: "2026.02",
104:         drop_from: "2026.08",
105:       )
106:       download(*, **)
107:     end
108: 
109:     def download!(*, **)
110:       perform_download(*, **)
111:     rescue StandardError
112:       raise DownloadError
113:     end
114: 
115:     def purge_tombstone(grace_period)
116:     end
117: 
118:     def get_path_for(type, id, sha, extension)
119:       depth = get_depth_for(id)
120:       tree = File.join(*sha[0, depth].chars, "")
121:       "#{type}/#{depth + 1}X/#{tree}#{sha}#{extension}"
122:     end
123: 
124:     def get_path_for_upload(upload)
125:       # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
126:       # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
127:       if upload.url.present? && (path = upload.url[UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX, 1])
128:         return prefix_path(path)
129:       end
130: 
131:       extension =
132:         if upload.extension
133:           ".#{upload.extension}"
134:         else
135:           # Maintain backward compatibility before Jobs::MigrateUploadExtensions runs
136:           File.extname(upload.original_filename)
137:         end
138: 
139:       get_path_for("original", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
140:     end
141: 
142:     def get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
143:       # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
144:       # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
145:       if optimized_image.url.present? && (path = optimized_image.url[OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX, 1])
146:         return prefix_path(path)
147:       end
148: 
149:       upload = optimized_image.upload
150:       version = optimized_image.version || 1
151:       extension =
152:         "_#{version}_#{optimized_image.width}x#{optimized_image.height}#{optimized_image.extension}"
153:       get_path_for("optimized", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
154:     end
155: 
156:     CACHE_DIR = "#{Rails.root}/tmp/download_cache/"
157:     CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE = 500
158:     CACHE_EVICT_COUNT = 100
159: 
160:     def get_cache_path_for(filename)
161:       "#{CACHE_DIR}#{filename}"
162:     end
163: 
164:     def get_from_cache(filename)
165:       path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
166:       path if File.exist?(path)
167:     end
168: 
169:     def cache_file(file, filename)
170:       path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
171:       dir = File.dirname(path)
172:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
173:       FileUtils.cp(file.path, path)
174: 
175:       DiskCacheEviction.evict(
176:         dir: CACHE_DIR,
177:         max_entries: CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE,
178:         evict_count: CACHE_EVICT_COUNT,
179:       )
180:     end
181: 
182:     private
183: 
184:     def perform_download(object, max_file_size_kb: nil)
185:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("download_#{object.sha1}", validity: 3.minutes) do
186:         name = object.respond_to?(:original_filename) ? object.original_filename : object.url
187:         extension = File.extname(name)
188:         filename = "#{object.sha1}#{extension}"
189: 
190:         unless File.exist?(get_cache_path_for(filename))
191:           max_file_size_kb ||= [
192:             SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
193:             SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
194:           ].max.kilobytes
195: 
196:           if object.respond_to?(:secure) ? object.secure? : object.upload.secure?
197:             url =
198:               Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(object.url, include_content_disposition: false)
199:           else
200:             url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(object.url)
201:           end
202: 
203:           url = "#{SiteSetting.scheme}:#{url}" if url =~ %r{\A//}
204: 
205:           file =
206:             FileHelper.download(
207:               url,
208:               max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,
209:               tmp_file_name: "discourse-download",
210:               follow_redirect: true,
211:             )
212: 
213:           return if file.nil?
214: 
215:           cache_file(file, filename)
216:         end
217: 
218:         get_from_cache(filename)
219:       end
220:     end
221: 
222:     def not_implemented
223:       raise "Not implemented."
224:     end
225: 
226:     def get_depth_for(id)
227:       depths = [0]
228:       depths << Math.log(id / 1_000.0, 16).ceil if id.positive?
229:       depths.max
230:     end
231: 
232:     def prefix_path(path)
233:       path
234:     end
235:   end
236: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "file_store/base_store"
4: 
5: module FileStore
6:   class LocalStore < BaseStore
7:     def store_file(file, path)
8:       copy_file(file, "#{public_dir}#{path}")
9:       "#{Discourse.base_path}#{path}"
10:     end
11: 
12:     def remove_file(url, _)
13:       return unless is_relative?(url)
14:       source = "#{public_dir}#{url}"
15:       return unless File.exist?(source)
16:       destination = "#{public_dir}#{url.sub("/uploads/", "/uploads/tombstone/")}"
17:       dir = Pathname.new(destination).dirname
18:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
19:       FileUtils.remove(destination) if File.exist?(destination)
20:       FileUtils.move(source, destination, force: true)
21:       FileUtils.touch(destination)
22:     end
23: 
24:     def has_been_uploaded?(url)
25:       is_relative?(url) || is_local?(url)
26:     end
27: 
28:     def absolute_base_url
29:       "#{Discourse.base_url_no_prefix}#{relative_base_url}"
30:     end
31: 
32:     def absolute_base_cdn_url
33:       "#{Discourse.asset_host}#{relative_base_url}"
34:     end
35: 
36:     def relative_base_url
37:       File.join(Discourse.base_path, upload_path)
38:     end
39: 
40:     def temporary_upload_path(filename)
41:       FileStore::BaseStore.temporary_upload_path(filename, folder_prefix: relative_base_url)
42:     end
43: 
44:     def external?
45:       false
46:     end
47: 
48:     def download_url(upload)
49:       return unless upload
50:       File.join(relative_base_url, upload.sha1)
51:     end
52: 
53:     def cdn_url(url)
54:       UrlHelper.local_cdn_url(url)
55:     end
56: 
57:     def path_for(upload)
58:       url = upload.try(:url)
59:       "#{public_dir}#{upload.url}" if url && url[0] == "/" && url[1] != "/"
60:     end
61: 
62:     def purge_tombstone(grace_period)
63:       if Dir.exist?(Discourse.store.tombstone_dir)
64:         Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
65:           "find",
66:           tombstone_dir,
67:           "-mtime",
68:           "+#{grace_period}",
69:           "-type",
70:           "f",
71:           "-delete",
72:         )
73:       end
74:     end
75: 
76:     def get_path_for(type, upload_id, sha, extension)
77:       prefix_path(super(type, upload_id, sha, extension))
78:     end
79: 
80:     def copy_file(file, path)
81:       dir = Pathname.new(path).dirname
82:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
83:       # move the file to the right location
84:       # not using mv, cause permissions are no good on move
85:       File.open(path, "wb") { |f| f.write(file.read) }
86:     end
87: 
88:     def is_relative?(url)
89:       url.present? && url.start_with?(relative_base_url)
90:     end
91: 
92:     def is_local?(url)
93:       return false if url.blank?
94:       absolute_url = url.start_with?("//") ? SiteSetting.scheme + ":" + url : url
95:       absolute_url.start_with?(absolute_base_url) || absolute_url.start_with?(absolute_base_cdn_url)
96:     end
97: 
98:     def public_dir
99:       File.join(Rails.root, "public")
100:     end
101: 
102:     def tombstone_dir
103:       "#{public_dir}#{relative_base_url.sub("/uploads/", "/uploads/tombstone/")}"
104:     end
105: 
106:     def list_missing_uploads(skip_optimized: false)
107:       list_missing(Upload)
108:       list_missing(OptimizedImage) unless skip_optimized
109:     end
110: 
111:     def copy_from(source_path)
112:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.join(public_dir, upload_path))
113: 
114:       Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
115:         "rsync",
116:         "-a",
117:         "--safe-links",
118:         "#{source_path}/",
119:         "#{upload_path}/",
120:         failure_message: "Failed to copy uploads.",
121:         chdir: public_dir,
122:       )
123:     end
124: 
125:     private
126: 
127:     def list_missing(model)
128:       count = 0
129:       model.find_each do |upload|
130:         # could be a remote image
131:         next unless upload.url =~ %r{\A/[^/]}
132: 
133:         path = "#{public_dir}#{upload.url}"
134:         bad = true
135:         begin
136:           bad = false if File.size(path) != 0
137:         rescue StandardError
138:           # something is messed up
139:         end
140:         if bad
141:           count += 1
142:           puts path
143:         end
144:       end
145:       puts "#{count} of #{model.count} #{model.name.underscore.pluralize} are missing" if count > 0
146:     end
147: 
148:     def prefix_path(path)
149:       File.join("/", upload_path, path)
150:     end
151:   end
152: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "uri"
4: require "mini_mime"
5: require "file_store/base_store"
6: require "s3_helper"
7: require "file_helper"
8: 
9: module FileStore
10:   class S3Store < BaseStore
11:     TOMBSTONE_PREFIX = "tombstone/"
12: 
13:     delegate :abort_multipart,
14:              :presign_multipart_part,
15:              :list_multipart_parts,
16:              :complete_multipart,
17:              to: :s3_helper
18: 
19:     def initialize(s3_helper = nil)
20:       @s3_helper = s3_helper
21:     end
22: 
23:     def s3_helper
24:       @s3_helper ||=
25:         S3Helper.new(
26:           s3_bucket,
27:           Rails.configuration.multisite ? multisite_tombstone_prefix : TOMBSTONE_PREFIX,
28:           use_accelerate_endpoint: SiteSetting.Upload.enable_s3_transfer_acceleration,
29:         )
30:     end
31: 
32:     def store_upload(file, upload, content_type = nil)
33:       upload.url = nil
34:       path = get_path_for_upload(upload)
35:       url, upload.etag =
36:         store_file(
37:           file,
38:           path,
39:           filename: upload.original_filename,
40:           content_type: content_type,
41:           cache_locally: true,
42:           private: upload.secure?,
43:           upload_id: upload.id,
44:         )
45:       url
46:     end
47: 
48:     def move_existing_stored_upload(existing_external_upload_key:, upload: nil, content_type: nil)
49:       upload.url = nil
50:       path = get_path_for_upload(upload)
51:       url, upload.etag =
52:         store_file(
53:           nil,
54:           path,
55:           filename: upload.original_filename,
56:           content_type: content_type,
57:           cache_locally: false,
58:           private: upload.secure?,
59:           move_existing: true,
60:           existing_external_upload_key: existing_external_upload_key,
61:         )
62:       url
63:     end
64: 
65:     def store_optimized_image(file, optimized_image, content_type = nil, secure: false)
66:       optimized_image.url = nil
67:       path = get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
68:       url, optimized_image.etag =
69:         store_file(file, path, content_type: content_type, private: secure)
70:       url
71:     end
72: 
73:     # File is an actual Tempfile on disk
74:     #
75:     # An existing_external_upload_key is given for cases where move_existing is specified.
76:     # This is an object already uploaded directly to S3 that we are now moving
77:     # to its final resting place with the correct sha and key.
78:     #
79:     # options
80:     #   - filename
81:     #   - content_type
82:     #   - cache_locally
83:     #   - move_existing
84:     #   - private
85:     #   - existing_external_upload_key
86:     def store_file(file, path, opts = {})
87:       path = path.dup
88: 
89:       filename = opts[:filename].presence || File.basename(path)
90:       # cache file locally when needed
91:       cache_file(file, File.basename(path)) if opts[:cache_locally]
92: 
93:       cache_control = "max-age=#{SiteSetting.s3_max_age}, public, immutable"
94:       if SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate != SiteSetting.defaults[:s3_stale_while_revalidate]
95:         cache_control =
96:           "#{cache_control}, stale-while-revalidate=#{SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate}"
97:       end
98: 
99:       options = {
100:         cache_control: cache_control,
101:         content_type:
102:           opts[:content_type].presence || MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(filename)&.content_type,
103:       }.merge(default_s3_options(secure: opts[:private]))
104: 
105:       # Only serve inline for allowlisted safe file types (non-SVG images and PDFs)
106:       # to prevent XSS via HTML/XML/SVG uploads. All other files force download.
107:       # See https://github.com/discourse/discourse/commit/31e31ef44973dc4daaee2f010d71588ea5873b53
108:       options[:content_disposition] = self.class.content_disposition_for(filename)
109: 
110:       path.prepend(File.join(upload_path, "/")) if Rails.configuration.multisite
111: 
112:       # if this fails, it will throw an exception
113:       if opts[:move_existing] && opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
114:         original_path = opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
115:         options[:apply_metadata_to_destination] = true
116:         path, etag = s3_helper.copy(original_path, path, options: options)
117:         delete_file(original_path)
118:       else
119:         path, etag = s3_helper.upload(file, path, options)
120:       end
121: 
122:       # return the upload url and etag
123:       [File.join(absolute_base_url, path), etag]
124:     end
125: 
126:     def delete_file(path)
127:       # delete the object outright without moving to tombstone,
128:       # not recommended for most use cases
129:       s3_helper.delete_object(path)
130:     end

132:     def remove_file(url, path)
133:       return unless has_been_uploaded?(url)
134:       # copy the removed file to tombstone
135:       s3_helper.remove(path, true)
136:     end

142:     def has_been_uploaded?(url)
143:       return false if url.blank?
144: 
145:       begin
146:         parsed_url = URI.parse(UrlHelper.encode(url))
147:       rescue StandardError
148:         # There are many exceptions possible here including Addressable::URI:: exceptions
149:         # and URI:: exceptions, catch all may seem wide, but it makes no sense to raise ever
150:         # on an invalid url here
151:         return false
152:       end
153: 
154:       base_hostname = URI.parse(absolute_base_url).hostname
155:       if url[base_hostname]
156:         # if the hostnames match it means the upload is in the same
157:         # bucket on s3. however, the bucket folder path may differ in
158:         # some cases, and we do not want to assume the url is uploaded
159:         # here. e.g. the path of the current site could be /prod and the
160:         # other site could be /staging
161:         if s3_bucket_folder_path.present?
162:           return parsed_url.path.starts_with?("/#{s3_bucket_folder_path}")
163:         else
164:           return true
165:         end
166:       end
167: 
168:       return false if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.blank?
169: 
170:       s3_cdn_url = URI.parse(SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url || "")
171:       cdn_hostname = s3_cdn_url.hostname
172: 
173:       if cdn_hostname.presence && url[cdn_hostname] &&
174:            (s3_cdn_url.path.blank? || parsed_url.path.starts_with?(s3_cdn_url.path))
175:         return true
176:       end
177:       false
178:     end

353:     def copy_from(source_path)
354:       local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
355:       public_upload_path = File.join(local_store.public_dir, local_store.upload_path)
356: 
357:       # The migration to S3 and lots of other code expects files to exist in public/uploads,
358:       # so lets move them there before executing the migration.
359:       if public_upload_path != source_path
360:         if Dir.exist?(public_upload_path)
361:           old_upload_path = "#{public_upload_path}_#{SecureRandom.hex}"
362:           FileUtils.mv(public_upload_path, old_upload_path)
363:         end
364:       end
365: 
366:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.expand_path("..", public_upload_path))
367:       FileUtils.symlink(source_path, public_upload_path)
368: 
369:       FileStore::ToS3Migration.new(
370:         s3_options: FileStore::ToS3Migration.s3_options_from_site_settings,
371:         migrate_to_multisite: Rails.configuration.multisite,
372:       ).migrate
373:     ensure
374:       FileUtils.rm(public_upload_path) if File.symlink?(public_upload_path)
375:       FileUtils.mv(old_upload_path, public_upload_path) if old_upload_path
376:     end

383:     # The following are canned ACLs defined by AWS S3 and not some generic value which we decide to use.
384:     # https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/acl-overview.html
385:     CANNED_ACL_PUBLIC_READ = "public-read"
386:     CANNED_ACL_PRIVATE = "private"

388:     def self.acl_option_value(secure:)
389:       return if !SiteSetting.s3_use_acls
390:       secure ? CANNED_ACL_PRIVATE : CANNED_ACL_PUBLIC_READ
391:     end

395:     end

397:     def self.visibility_tagging_option_value(secure:, encode_form: true)
398:       return if !SiteSetting.s3_enable_access_control_tags
399: 
400:       key = SiteSetting.s3_access_control_tag_key
401:       return if key.blank?
402: 
403:       option_value = {
404:         key =>
405:           (
406:             if secure
407:               SiteSetting.s3_access_control_tag_private_value
408:             else
409:               SiteSetting.s3_access_control_tag_public_value
410:             end
411:           ),
412:       }
413: 
414:       encode_form ? URI.encode_www_form(option_value) : option_value
415:     end
416: 
417:     def self.default_s3_options(secure:)
418:       options = {}
419: 
420:       if acl_value = acl_option_value(secure:)
421:         options[:acl] = acl_value
422:       end
423: 
424:       if tagging_option_value = visibility_tagging_option_value(secure:)
425:         options[:tagging] = tagging_option_value
426:       end
427: 
428:       options
429:     end

431:     def default_s3_options(secure:)
432:       self.class.default_s3_options(secure:)
433:     end
434: 
435:     # S3 limits total request header/metadata size to 2 KB. Content-Disposition
436:     # includes the filename twice (filename= and filename*=UTF-8''), so we
437:     # limit the full header to 1600 bytes to stay within that budget.
438:     MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES = 1_600
439: 
440:     def self.content_disposition_for(filename, disposition: nil)
441:       disposition ||= FileHelper.is_inline_safe?(filename) ? "inline" : "attachment"
442:       return "" if filename.blank?
443: 
444:       header = ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(disposition:, filename:)
445:       return header if header.bytesize <= MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES
446: 
447:       extension = File.extname(filename)
448:       basename = File.basename(filename, extension)
449: 
450:       # Binary search for the longest basename that fits within the limit
451:       low = 1
452:       high = basename.length
453: 
454:       while low < high
455:         mid = (low + high + 1) / 2
456:         candidate = "#{basename[0...mid]}#{extension}"
457:         test_header =
458:           ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(disposition:, filename: candidate)
459:         if test_header.bytesize <= MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES
460:           low = mid
461:         else
462:           high = mid - 1
463:         end
464:       end
465: 
466:       ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(
467:         disposition:,
468:         filename: "#{basename[0...low]}#{extension}",
469:       )
470:     end

473: 
474:     def presigned_get_url(
475:       url,
476:       force_download: false,
477:       filename: false,
478:       expires_in: SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds
479:     )
480:       opts = { expires_in: expires_in }
481: 
482:       if filename
483:         disposition =
484:           (force_download || !FileHelper.is_inline_safe?(filename)) ? "attachment" : "inline"
485:         opts[:response_content_disposition] = ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(
486:           disposition:,
487:           filename:,
488:         )
489:       end
490: 
491:       obj = object_from_path(url)
492:       obj.presigned_url(:get, opts)
493:     end
494: 
495:     def get_upload_key(upload)
496:       if Rails.configuration.multisite
497:         File.join(upload_path, "/", get_path_for_upload(upload))
498:       else
499:         get_path_for_upload(upload)
500:       end
501:     end
502: 
503:     def update_access_control(key, secure, remove_existing_acl: false)
504:       acl = self.class.acl_option_value(secure:)
505: 
506:       if acl.present? || remove_existing_acl
507:         begin
508:           object_from_path(key).acl.put(acl:)
509:         rescue Aws::S3::Errors::NotImplemented => err
510:           Discourse.warn_exception(
511:             err,
512:             message: "The file store object storage provider does not support setting ACLs",
513:           )
514:         end
515:       end
516: 
517:       if tagging_option_value =
518:            self.class.visibility_tagging_option_value(secure:, encode_form: false)
519:         s3_helper.upsert_tag(
520:           key,
521:           tag_key: tagging_option_value.keys.first,
522:           tag_value: tagging_option_value.values.first,
523:         )
524:       end
525:     rescue Aws::S3::Errors::NoSuchKey
526:       Rails.logger.warn(
527:         "Could not update access control on upload with key: '#{key}'. Upload is missing.",
528:       )
529:     end

531:     def list_missing(model, prefix)
532:       connection = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.raw_connection
533:       connection.exec("CREATE TEMP TABLE verified_ids(val integer PRIMARY KEY)")
534:       marker = nil
535:       files = s3_helper.list(prefix, marker)
536: 
537:       while files.count > 0
538:         verified_ids = []
539: 
540:         files.each do |f|
541:           id = model.where("url LIKE '%#{f.key}' AND etag = '#{f.etag}'").pick(:id)
542:           verified_ids << id if id.present?
543:           marker = f.key
544:         end
545: 
546:         verified_id_clause =
547:           verified_ids.map { |id| "('#{PG::Connection.escape_string(id.to_s)}')" }.join(",")
548:         connection.exec("INSERT INTO verified_ids VALUES #{verified_id_clause}")
549:         files = s3_helper.list(prefix, marker)
550:       end
551: 
552:       missing_uploads =
553:         model.joins("LEFT JOIN verified_ids ON verified_ids.val = id").where(
554:           "verified_ids.val IS NULL",
555:         )
556:       missing_count = missing_uploads.count
557: 
558:       if missing_count > 0
559:         missing_uploads.find_each { |upload| puts upload.url }
560: 
561:         puts "#{missing_count} of #{model.count} #{model.name.underscore.pluralize} are missing"
562:       end
563:     ensure
564:       connection.exec("DROP TABLE verified_ids") unless connection.nil?
565:     end
566:   end
567: end

3: class OptimizedImage < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   include HasUrl
5:   belongs_to :upload
6: 
7:   # BUMP UP if optimized image algorithm changes
8:   VERSION = 2
9:   URL_REGEX = %r{(/optimized/\dX[/\.\w]*/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)[\.\w]*)}
10: 
11:   def self.lock(upload_id, width, height)
12:     @hostname ||= Discourse.os_hostname
13:     # note, the extra lock here ensures we only optimize one image per machine on webs
14:     # this can very easily lead to runaway CPU so slowing it down is beneficial and it is hijacked
15:     #
16:     # we can not afford this blocking in Sidekiq cause it can lead to starvation
17:     if lock_per_machine?
18:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_host_#{@hostname}") do
19:         DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
20:       end
21:     else
22:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
23:     end
24:   end
25: 
26:   def self.lock_per_machine?
27:     return @lock_per_machine if defined?(@lock_per_machine)
28:     @lock_per_machine = !Sidekiq.server?
29:   end
30: 
31:   def self.lock_per_machine=(value)
32:     @lock_per_machine = value
33:   end
34: 
35:   def self.create_for(upload, width, height, opts = {})
36:     return if width <= 0 || height <= 0
37:     return if upload.try(:sha1).blank?
38: 
39:     # no extension so try to guess it
40:     upload.fix_image_extension if (!upload.extension)
41: 
42:     if !upload.extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
43:       if opts[:raise_on_error]
44:         raise InvalidAccess
45:       else
46:         # nothing to do ... bad extension, not an image
47:         return
48:       end
49:     end
50: 
51:     # prefer to look up the thumbnail without grabbing any locks
52:     extension = ".#{opts[:format] || upload.extension}"
53:     thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
54: 
55:     # correct bad thumbnail if needed
56:     if thumbnail && (thumbnail.url.blank? || thumbnail.version != VERSION)
57:       thumbnail.destroy!
58:       thumbnail = nil
59:     end
60: 
61:     return thumbnail if thumbnail
62: 
63:     store = Discourse.store
64: 
65:     # create the thumbnail otherwise
66:     original_path = store.path_for(upload)
67: 
68:     if original_path.blank?
69:       # download is protected with a DistributedMutex
70:       original_path = store.download(upload)
71:     end
72: 
73:     if extension == ".svg" && upload.extension != "svg"
74:       if opts[:raise_on_error]
75:         raise InvalidAccess
76:       else
77:         # we can not convert any images to svg, unsupported
78:         return
79:       end
80:     end
81: 
82:     lock(upload.id, width, height) do
83:       # may have been generated since we got the lock
84:       thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
85: 
86:       # return the previous thumbnail if any
87:       return thumbnail if thumbnail
88: 
89:       if original_path.blank?
90:         Rails.logger.error("Could not find file in the store located at url: #{upload.url}")
91:       else
92:         # create a temp file with the same extension as the original
93: 
94:         return nil if extension.length == 1
95: 
96:         temp_file = Tempfile.new(["discourse-thumbnail", extension])
97:         temp_path = temp_file.path
98: 
99:         target_quality =
100:           upload.target_image_quality(
101:             original_path,
102:             SiteSetting.ImageQuality.image_preview_jpg_quality,
103:           )
104:         opts = opts.merge(quality: target_quality) if target_quality
105:         opts = opts.merge(upload_id: upload.id)
106: 
107:         # special case, when "resizing" vectors we simply copy
108:         if extension == ".svg"
109:           FileUtils.cp(original_path, temp_path)
110:           resized = true
111:         elsif opts[:crop]
112:           resized = crop(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
113:         else
114:           resized = resize(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
115:         end
116: 
117:         if resized
118:           # TODO: crop vs resize should be stored in the db, quality should be stored
119:           thumbnail =
120:             OptimizedImage.create!(
121:               upload_id: upload.id,
122:               sha1: Upload.generate_digest(temp_path),
123:               extension: extension,
124:               width: width,
125:               height: height,
126:               url: "",

186:   def self.safe_path?(path)
187:     # this matches instructions which call #to_s
188:     path = path.to_s
189:     return false if path != File.expand_path(path)
190:     return false if path !~ %r{\A[\w\-\./]+\z}m

195:     paths.each { |path| raise Discourse::InvalidAccess unless safe_path?(path) }

198:   IM_DECODERS = /\A(jpe?g|png|ico|gif|webp|avif|svg)\z/i
199: 
200:   def self.prepend_decoder!(path, ext_path = nil, opts = nil)
201:     opts ||= {}
202: 
203:     # This logic is a little messy but the result of using mocks for most
204:     # of the image tests. The idea here is you shouldn't trust the "original"
205:     # path of a file to figure out its extension. However, in certain cases
206:     # such as generating the loading upload thumbnail, we force the format,
207:     # and this allows us to use the forced format in that case.
208:     extension = nil
209:     if (opts[:format] && path != ext_path)
210:       extension = File.extname(path)[1..-1]
211:     else
212:       extension = File.extname(opts[:filename] || ext_path || path)[1..-1]
213:     end
214: 
215:     if !extension || !extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
216:       raise Discourse::InvalidAccess.new("Unsupported extension: #{extension}")
217:     end
218:     "#{extension}:#{path}"
219:   end

225:   def self.resize_instructions(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
226:     ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
227: 
228:     # note FROM my not be named correctly
229:     from = prepend_decoder!(from, to, opts)
230:     to = prepend_decoder!(to, to, opts)
231: 
232:     instructions = ["convert", "#{from}[0]"]
233: 
234:     instructions << "-colors" << opts[:colors].to_s if opts[:colors]
235: 
236:     instructions << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
237: 
238:     # NOTE: ORDER is important!
239:     instructions.concat(
240:       %W[
241:         -auto-orient
242:         -gravity
243:         center

331:     method_name = "#{operation}_instructions"
332: 
333:     instructions = self.public_send(method_name.to_sym, from, to, dimensions, opts)
334:     convert_with(instructions, to, opts)

338:   MAX_CONVERT_SECONDS = 20

340:   def self.convert_with(instructions, to, opts = {})
341:     Discourse::Utils.execute_command(

343:       "-n",
344:       "10",

346:       timeout: MAX_CONVERT_SECONDS,
347:     )
348: 
349:     allow_pngquant = to.downcase.ends_with?(".png") && File.size(to) < MAX_PNGQUANT_SIZE

351:     true

367:         location: to,

369:         instructions: instructions,

371:       false
372:     end
373:   end

21:       .find_each do |user_export|
22:         UserExport.transaction do
23:           begin
24:             Post.where(topic_id: user_export.topic_id).find_each { |p| p.destroy! }

32:       end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ThemeField < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   MIGRATION_NAME_PART_MAX_LENGTH = 150
5: 
6:   # This string is not 'secret'. It's just randomized to avoid accidental
7:   # clashes with genuine theme field content.
8:   CSP_NONCE_PLACEHOLDER = "__CSP__NONCE__PLACEHOLDER__f72bff1b1768168a34ee092ce759f192__"
9: 
10:   belongs_to :upload
11:   has_one :javascript_cache, -> { where(name: nil) }, dependent: :destroy, autosave: true
12:   has_many :raw_javascript_caches,
13:            -> { where.not(name: nil) },
14:            class_name: "JavascriptCache",
15:            dependent: :destroy,
16:            autosave: true
17:   has_one :upload_reference, as: :target, dependent: :destroy
18:   has_one :theme_settings_migration, dependent: :destroy
19: 
20:   validates :value, { length: { maximum: 1024**2 } }
21: 
22:   validate :migration_filename_is_valid, if: :migration_field?
23: 
24:   after_save do
25:     if (
26:          self.type_id == ThemeField.types[:theme_screenshot_upload_var] ||
27:            self.type_id == ThemeField.types[:theme_upload_var]
28:        ) && saved_change_to_upload_id?
29:       UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [self.upload_id], target: self)
30:     end
31:   end
32: 
33:   after_save do
34:     dependent_fields.each(&:invalidate_baked!)
35: 
36:     if upload && svg_sprite_field?
37:       upsert_svg_sprite!
38:       SvgSprite.expire_cache
39:     end
40:   end
41: 
42:   after_destroy do
43:     if svg_sprite_field?
44:       ThemeSvgSprite.where(theme_id: theme_id).delete_all
45: 
46:       SvgSprite.expire_cache
47:     end
48:   end
49: 
50:   scope :find_by_theme_ids,
51:         ->(theme_ids) do
52:           return none if theme_ids.blank?
53: 
54:           where(theme_id: theme_ids).joins(
55:             "JOIN (
56:           SELECT #{theme_ids.map.with_index { |id, idx| "#{id.to_i} AS theme_id, #{idx} AS theme_sort_column" }.join(" UNION ALL SELECT ")}
57:         ) as X ON X.theme_id = theme_fields.theme_id",
58:           ).order("theme_sort_column")
59:         end
60: 
61:   scope :filter_locale_fields,
62:         ->(locale_codes) do
63:           return none if locale_codes.blank?
64: 
65:           where(target_id: Theme.targets[:translations], name: locale_codes).joins(
66:             DB.sql_fragment(
67:               "JOIN (
68:         SELECT * FROM (VALUES #{locale_codes.map { "(?)" }.join(",")}) as Y (locale_code, locale_sort_column)
69:       ) as Y ON Y.locale_code = theme_fields.name",
70:               *locale_codes.map.with_index { |code, index| [code, index] },
71:             ),
72:           ).order("Y.locale_sort_column")
73:         end
74: 
75:   scope :find_first_locale_fields,
76:         ->(theme_ids, locale_codes) do
77:           find_by_theme_ids(theme_ids)
78:             .filter_locale_fields(locale_codes)
79:             .reorder("X.theme_sort_column", "Y.locale_sort_column")
80:             .select("DISTINCT ON (X.theme_sort_column) *")
81:         end
82: 
83:   scope :svg_sprite_fields,
84:         -> do
85:           where(type_id: ThemeField.theme_var_type_ids, name: SvgSprite.theme_sprite_variable_name)
86:         end
87: 
88:   def self.types
89:     @types ||=
90:       Enum.new(
91:         html: 0,
92:         scss: 1,
93:         theme_upload_var: 2,
94:         theme_color_var: 3, # No longer used
95:         theme_var: 4, # No longer used
96:         yaml: 5,
97:         js: 6,
98:         theme_screenshot_upload_var: 7,
99:         json: 8,
100:       )
101:   end
102: 
103:   def self.theme_var_type_ids
104:     @theme_var_type_ids ||= [2]
105:   end
106: 
107:   def self.css_theme_type_ids
108:     @css_theme_type_ids ||= [0, 1]
109:   end
110: 
111:   def self.force_recompilation!
112:     find_each do |field|
113:       field.compiler_version = 0
114:       field.ensure_baked!
115:     end
116:   end
117: 
118:   validates :name,
119:             format: {
120:               with: /\A[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*\z/i,
121:             },
122:             if: Proc.new { |field| ThemeField.theme_var_type_ids.include?(field.type_id) }
123: 
124:   belongs_to :theme
125: 
126:   def process_html(html)
127:     errors = []
128:     errors << I18n.t("themes.errors.optimized_link") if contains_optimized_link?(html)
129: 
130:     doc = Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(html)
131: 
132:     unused_raw_caches = Set.new raw_javascript_caches.to_a
133: 
134:     doc
135:       .css("script")
136:       .select { |node| inline_javascript?(node) }
137:       .each_with_index do |node, index|
138:         unique_name =
139:           "theme-#{theme_id}-inline-#{Theme.targets[self.target_id]}-#{name}#{"-#{index + 1}" if index > 0}"
140: 
141:         cache =
142:           raw_javascript_caches.find { |c| c.name == unique_name } ||
143:             raw_javascript_caches.build(name: unique_name)
144:         transpiled =
145:           begin
146:             AssetProcessor.new(skip_module: true).perform(
147:               node.inner_html,
148:               nil,
149:               "theme-#{theme_id}/#{unique_name}.js",
150:               generate_map: true,
151:             )
152:           rescue AssetProcessor::TranspileError => e
153:             message = "[THEME #{theme_id} '#{theme.name}'] Compile error: #{e.message}"
154:             errors << message
155:             { "code" => "console.error(#{message.to_json});\n", "map" => nil }
156:           end
157:         cache.content = transpiled["code"]
158:         cache.source_map = transpiled["map"]
159:         cache.save!
160:         unused_raw_caches.delete(cache)
161: 
162:         node.replace "<script defer src='#{cache.url}' data-theme-id='#{theme_id}'></script>"
163:       end
164: 
165:     doc.css("script").each { |node| node["nonce"] = CSP_NONCE_PLACEHOLDER }
166: 
167:     unused_raw_caches.each(&:destroy!)
168: 
169:     [doc.to_s, errors&.join("\n")]
170:   end
171: 
172:   def validate_svg_sprite_xml
173:     upload = Upload.find_by(id: self.upload_id)
174: 
175:     return "Error with #{self.name}: No upload found for the provided upload_id" if upload.nil?
176: 
177:     if Discourse.store.external?
178:       path = Discourse.store.download(upload)
179:     else
180:       path = Discourse.store.path_for(upload)
181:     end
182: 
183:     return "Error with #{self.name}: Could not download icon sprite" if path.nil?
184: 
185:     error = nil
186: 
187:     begin
188:       content = File.read(path)
189:       if content.to_s.bytesize > SvgSprite::MAX_THEME_SPRITE_SIZE
190:         error = "Error with #{self.name}: Icon sprite file is too large"
191:       else
192:         Nokogiri.XML(content) { |config| config.options = Nokogiri::XML::ParseOptions::NOBLANKS }
193:       end
194:     rescue => e
195:       error = "Error with #{self.name}: #{e.inspect}"
196:     end
197: 
198:     error
199:   end
