1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ThemeSetting < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   belongs_to :theme
5: 
6:   has_many :upload_references, as: :target, dependent: :destroy
7: 
8:   TYPES_ENUM =
9:     Enum.new(integer: 0, float: 1, string: 2, bool: 3, list: 4, enum: 5, upload: 6, objects: 7)
10: 
11:   MAXIMUM_JSON_VALUE_SIZE_BYTES = 0.5 * 1024 * 1024 # 0.5 MB
12: 
13:   validates :name, :theme, presence: true
14:   validates :data_type, inclusion: { in: TYPES_ENUM.values }
15:   validate :json_value_size, if: -> { self.data_type == TYPES_ENUM[:objects] }
16:   validates :name, length: { maximum: 255 }
17: 
18:   after_destroy :clear_settings_cache
19:   after_save :clear_settings_cache
20: 
21:   after_save do
22:     if self.data_type == ThemeSetting.types[:upload] && saved_change_to_value?
23:       UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [self.value], target: self)
24:     elsif self.data_type == ThemeSetting.types[:objects] && saved_change_to_json_value? &&
25:           self.json_value.present?
26:       upload_values =
27:         SchemaSettingsObjectValidator.property_values_of_type(
28:           schema: theme.settings[self.name.to_sym].schema,
29:           objects: self.json_value,
30:           type: "upload",
31:         )
32: 
33:       # Convert URLs to upload IDs (values can be either integer IDs or URL strings)
34:       upload_ids =
35:         upload_values.filter_map do |value|
36:           if value.is_a?(Integer)
37:             value
38:           elsif value.is_a?(String) && value.present?
39:             Upload.get_from_url(value)&.id
40:           end
41:         end
42: 
43:       # Always call ensure_exist! to clean up old references when uploads are removed
44:       UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self)
45:     end
46: 
47:     if theme.theme_modifier_set.refresh_theme_setting_modifiers(
48:          target_setting_name: self.name,
49:          target_setting_value: self.value,
50:        )
51:       theme.theme_modifier_set.save!
52:     end
53:   end
54: 
55:   def clear_settings_cache
56:     # All necessary caches will be cleared on next ensure_baked!
57:     theme.settings_field&.invalidate_baked!
58:   end
59: 
60:   def self.types
61:     TYPES_ENUM
62:   end
63: 
64:   def self.guess_type(value)
65:     case value
66:     when Integer
67:       types[:integer]
68:     when Float
69:       types[:float]
70:     when String
71:       types[:string]
72:     when TrueClass, FalseClass
73:       types[:bool]
74:     end
75:   end
76: 
77:   private
78: 
79:   def json_value_size
80:     if json_value.to_json.size > MAXIMUM_JSON_VALUE_SIZE_BYTES
81:       errors.add(
82:         :json_value,
83:         I18n.t(
84:           "theme_settings.errors.json_value.too_large",
85:           max_size: MAXIMUM_JSON_VALUE_SIZE_BYTES / 1024 / 1024,
86:         ),
87:       )
88:     end
89:   end
90: end
91: 
92: # == Schema Information
93: #
94: # Table name: theme_settings
95: #
96: #  id         :bigint           not null, primary key
97: #  name       :string(255)      not null
98: #  data_type  :integer          not null
99: #  value      :text
100: #  theme_id   :integer          not null
101: #  created_at :datetime         not null
102: #  updated_at :datetime         not null
103: #  json_value :jsonb
104: #

87: 
88:   after_save do
89:     if saved_change_to_value?
90:       if self.data_type == SiteSettings::TypeSupervisor.types[:upload]
91:         UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [self.value], target: self)
92:       elsif self.data_type == SiteSettings::TypeSupervisor.types[:uploaded_image_list]
93:         upload_ids = self.value.split("|").compact.uniq
94:         UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self)
95:       elsif self.data_type == SiteSettings::TypeSupervisor.types[:objects] && self.value.present?
96:         upload_values =
97:           SchemaSettingsObjectValidator.property_values_of_type(
98:             schema: SiteSetting.type_supervisor.type_hash(self.name.to_sym)[:schema],
99:             objects: JSON.parse(self.value),
100:             type: "upload",
101:           )
102: 
103:         # Convert URLs to upload IDs (values can be either integer IDs or URL strings)
104:         upload_ids =
105:           upload_values.filter_map do |value|
106:             if value.is_a?(Integer)
107:               value
108:             elsif value.is_a?(String) && value.present?
109:               ::Upload.get_from_url(value)&.id
110:             end
111:           end
112: 
113:         # Always call ensure_exist! to clean up old references when uploads are removed
114:         UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self)
115:       end
116:     end
117:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # Post processing that we can do after a post has already been cooked.
4: # For example, inserting the onebox content, or image sizes/thumbnails.
5: 
6: class CookedPostProcessor
7:   include CookedProcessorMixin
8: 
9:   attr_reader :cooking_options, :doc
10: 
11:   def initialize(post, opts = {})
12:     @dirty = false
13:     @opts = opts
14:     @post = post
15:     @model = post
16:     @previous_cooked = (@post.cooked || "").dup
17:     # NOTE: we re-cook the post here in order to prevent timing issues with edits
18:     # cf. https://meta.discourse.org/t/edit-of-rebaked-post-doesnt-show-in-html-only-in-raw/33815/6
19:     @cooking_options = post.cooking_options || opts[:cooking_options] || {}
20:     @cooking_options[:topic_id] = post.topic_id
21:     @cooking_options = @cooking_options.symbolize_keys
22:     @should_secure_uploads = @post.should_secure_uploads?
23:     @category_id = @post&.topic&.category_id
24: 
25:     cooked = post.cook(post.raw, @cooking_options)
26:     @doc = Loofah.html5_fragment(cooked)
27:     @has_oneboxes = post.post_analyzer.found_oneboxes?
28:     @size_cache = {}
29: 
30:     @disable_dominant_color = !!opts[:disable_dominant_color]
31:     @omit_nofollow = post.omit_nofollow?
32:   end
33: 
34:   def post_process(new_post: false)
35:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("post_process_#{@post.id}", validity: 10.minutes) do
36:       DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_post_process_cooked, @doc, @post)
37:       update_uploads_secure_status
38:       remove_full_quote_on_direct_reply if new_post
39:       post_process_oneboxes
40:       post_process_images
41:       post_process_videos
42:       add_blocked_hotlinked_media_placeholders
43:       post_process_quotes
44:       optimize_urls
45:       remove_user_ids
46:       update_post_image
47:       enforce_nofollow
48:       grant_badges
49:       @post.link_post_uploads(fragments: @doc)
50:       DiscourseEvent.trigger(:post_process_cooked, @doc, @post)
51:       nil
52:     end
53:   end
54: 
55:   def has_emoji?
56:     (@doc.css("img.emoji") - @doc.css(".quote img")).size > 0
57:   end
58: 
59:   def grant_badges
60:     return if @post.user.blank? || !Guardian.new.can_see?(@post)
61: 
62:     BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstEmoji), @post.user, post_id: @post.id) if has_emoji?
63:     if @has_oneboxes
64:       BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstOnebox), @post.user, post_id: @post.id)
65:     end
66:     if @post.is_reply_by_email?
67:       BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstReplyByEmail), @post.user, post_id: @post.id)
68:     end
69:   end
70: 
71:   def post_process_quotes
72:     @doc
73:       .css("aside.quote")
74:       .each do |q|
75:         post_number = q["data-post"]
76:         topic_id = q["data-topic"]
77:         if topic_id && post_number
78:           comparer = QuoteComparer.new(topic_id.to_i, post_number.to_i, q.css("blockquote").text)
79: 
80:           q["class"] = ((q["class"] || "") + " quote-post-not-found").strip if comparer.missing?
81:           q["class"] = ((q["class"] || "") + " quote-modified").strip if comparer.modified?
82:         end
83:       end
84:   end
85: 
86:   def update_uploads_secure_status
87:     @post.update_uploads_secure_status(source: "post processor")
88:   end
89: 
90:   def remove_full_quote_on_direct_reply
91:     return if !SiteSetting.remove_full_quote
92:     return if @post.post_number == 1
93:     return if @doc.xpath("aside[contains(@class, 'quote')]").size != 1
94: 
95:     previous =
96:       Post
97:         .where(
98:           "post_number < ? AND topic_id = ? AND post_type = ? AND NOT hidden",
99:           @post.post_number,
100:           @post.topic_id,
101:           Post.types[:regular],
102:         )
103:         .order("post_number DESC")
104:         .limit(1)
105:         .pluck(:cooked)
106:         .first
107: 
108:     return if previous.blank?
109: 
110:     previous_text = Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(previous).text.strip
111:     quoted_text = @doc.css("aside.quote:first-child blockquote").first&.text&.strip || ""
112: 
113:     return if previous_text.gsub(/(\s){2,}/, '\1') != quoted_text.gsub(/(\s){2,}/, '\1')
114: 
115:     quote_regexp = %r{\A\s*\[quote.+\[/quote\]}im
116:     quoteless_raw = @post.raw.sub(quote_regexp, "").strip
117: 
118:     return if @post.raw.strip == quoteless_raw
119: 
120:     PostRevisor.new(@post).revise!(
121:       Discourse.system_user,
122:       { raw: quoteless_raw, edit_reason: I18n.t(:removed_direct_reply_full_quotes) },
123:       skip_validations: true,
124:       bypass_bump: true,
125:     )
126:   end
127: 
128:   def extract_images_for_post
129:     # all images with a src attribute
130:     @doc.css("img[src]") -
131:       # minus emojis
132:       @doc.css("img.emoji") -
133:       # minus images inside quotes
134:       @doc.css(".quote img") -
135:       # minus onebox site icons
136:       @doc.css("img.site-icon") -
137:       # minus onebox avatars
138:       @doc.css("img.onebox-avatar") - @doc.css("img.onebox-avatar-inline") -
139:       # minus github onebox profile images
140:       @doc.css(".onebox.githubfolder img")
141:   end
142: 
143:   def update_post_image
144:     upload = nil
145:     images = extract_images_for_post
146: 
147:     @post.each_upload_url(fragments: images.css("[data-thumbnail]")) do |url, path, sha1|
148:       upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
149:       break if upload
150:     end
151: 
152:     if upload.nil? # No specified thumbnail. Use any image:
153:       @post.each_upload_url(fragments: images.css(":not([data-thumbnail])")) do |url, path, sha1|
154:         upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
155:         break if upload
156:       end
157:     end
158: 
159:     if upload.present?
160:       @post.update_column(:image_upload_id, upload.id) # post
161:       if @post.is_first_post? # topic
162:         @post.topic.update_column(:image_upload_id, upload.id)
163:         extra_sizes =
164:           ThemeModifierHelper.new(theme_ids: Theme.user_selectable.pluck(:id)).topic_thumbnail_sizes
165:         @post.topic.generate_thumbnails!(extra_sizes: extra_sizes)
166:       end
167:     else
168:       @post.update_column(:image_upload_id, nil) if @post.image_upload_id
169:       if @post.topic.image_upload_id && @post.is_first_post?
170:         @post.topic.update_column(:image_upload_id, nil)
171:       end
172:       nil
173:     end
174:   end
175: 
176:   def optimize_urls
177:     %w[href data-download-href].each do |selector|
178:       @doc.css("a[#{selector}]").each { |a| a[selector] = UrlHelper.cook_url(a[selector].to_s) }
179:     end
180: 
181:     %w[src].each do |selector|
182:       @doc
183:         .css("img[#{selector}], video[#{selector}]")
184:         .each do |img|
185:           custom_emoji = img["class"]&.include?("emoji-custom") && Emoji.custom?(img["title"])
186:           img[selector] = UrlHelper.cook_url(
187:             img[selector].to_s,
188:             secure: @should_secure_uploads && !custom_emoji,
189:           )
190:         end
191:     end
192:   end
193: 
194:   def remove_user_ids
195:     @doc
196:       .css("a[href]")
197:       .each do |a|
198:         uri =
199:           begin
200:             URI(a["href"])
201:           rescue URI::Error
202:             next
203:           end
204:         next if uri.hostname != Discourse.current_hostname
205: 
206:         query = Rack::Utils.parse_nested_query(uri.query)
207:         next if !query.delete("u")
208: 
209:         uri.query = query.map { |k, v| "#{k}=#{v}" }.join("&").presence
210:         a["href"] = uri.to_s
211:       end
212:   end
213: 
214:   def enforce_nofollow
215:     add_nofollow = !@omit_nofollow && SiteSetting.add_rel_nofollow_to_user_content
216:     PrettyText.add_rel_attributes_to_user_content(@doc, add_nofollow)
217:   end
218: 
219:   private
220:

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module CookedProcessorMixin
4:   GIF_SOURCES_REGEXP = %r{(giphy|tenor)\.com/}
5:   LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS = "lightbox-wrapper"
6:   MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH = 100
7:   MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT = 100
8: 
9:   def post_process_oneboxes
10:     limit = SiteSetting.max_oneboxes_per_post - @doc.css("aside.onebox, a.inline-onebox").size
11:     oneboxes = {}
12:     inlineOneboxes = {}
13: 
14:     Oneboxer.apply(@doc, extra_paths: [".inline-onebox-loading"]) do |url, element|
15:       is_onebox = element["class"] == Oneboxer::ONEBOX_CSS_CLASS
16:       map = is_onebox ? oneboxes : inlineOneboxes
17:       skip_onebox = limit <= 0 && !map[url]
18: 
19:       if skip_onebox
20:         if is_onebox
21:           element.remove_class("onebox")
22:         else
23:           remove_inline_onebox_loading_class(element)
24:         end
25: 
26:         next
27:       end
28: 
29:       limit -= 1
30:       map[url] = true
31: 
32:       if is_onebox
33:         onebox =
34:           Oneboxer.onebox(
35:             url,
36:             invalidate_oneboxes: !!@opts[:invalidate_oneboxes],
37:             user_id: @model&.user_id,
38:             category_id: @category_id,
39:           )
40: 
41:         @has_oneboxes = true if onebox.present?
42:         onebox
43:       else
44:         process_inline_onebox(element)
45:         false
46:       end
47:     end
48: 
49:     PrettyText.sanitize_hotlinked_media(@doc)
50: 
51:     oneboxed_images.each do |img|
52:       next if img["src"].blank?
53: 
54:       parent = img.parent
55: 
56:       if respond_to?(:process_hotlinked_image, true)
57:         still_an_image = process_hotlinked_image(img)
58:         next if !still_an_image
59:       end
60: 
61:       # make sure we grab dimensions for oneboxed images
62:       # and wrap in a div
63:       limit_size!(img)
64: 
65:       next if img["class"]&.include?("onebox-avatar")
66: 
67:       parent = parent&.parent if parent&.name == "a"
68:       parent_class = parent && parent["class"]
69:       width = img["width"].to_i
70:       height = img["height"].to_i
71: 
72:       if parent_class&.include?("onebox-body") && width > 0 && height > 0
73:         # special instruction for width == height, assume we are dealing with an avatar
74:         if (img["width"].to_i == img["height"].to_i)
75:           found = false
76:           parent = img
77:           while parent = parent.parent
78:             if parent["class"] && parent["class"].match?(/\b(allowlistedgeneric|discoursetopic)\b/)
79:               found = true
80:               break
81:             end
82:           end
83: 
84:           if found
85:             img["class"] = img["class"].to_s + " onebox-avatar"
86:             next
87:           end
88:         end
89: 
90:         if width < 64 && height < 64
91:           img["class"] = img["class"].to_s + " onebox-full-image"
92:         else
93:           img.delete("width")
94:           img.delete("height")
95:           new_parent =
96:             img.add_next_sibling(
97:               "<div class='aspect-image' style='--aspect-ratio:#{width}/#{height};'/>",
98:             )
99:           new_parent.first.add_child(img)
100:         end
101:       elsif (
102:             parent_class&.include?("instagram-images") || parent_class&.include?("tweet-images") ||
103:               parent_class&.include?("scale-images")
104:           ) && width > 0 && height > 0
105:         img.remove_attribute("width")
106:         img.remove_attribute("height")
107:         parent["class"] = "aspect-image-full-size"
108:         parent["style"] = "--aspect-ratio:#{width}/#{height};"
109:       end
110:     end
111: 
112:     if @omit_nofollow || !SiteSetting.add_rel_nofollow_to_user_content
113:       @doc
114:         .css(".onebox-body a[rel], .onebox a[rel]")
115:         .each do |a|
116:           rel_values = a["rel"].split(" ").map(&:downcase)
117:           rel_values.delete("nofollow")
118:           rel_values.delete("ugc")
119:           if rel_values.blank?
120:             a.remove_attribute("rel")
121:           else
122:             a["rel"] = rel_values.join(" ")
123:           end
124:         end
125:     end
126:   end
127: 
128:   def post_process_images
129:     extract_images.each do |img|
130:       still_an_image = process_hotlinked_image(img)
131:       convert_to_link!(img) if still_an_image
132:     end
133:   end
134: 
135:   def extract_images
136:     # all images with a src attribute
137:     @doc.css("img[src], img[#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR}]") -
138:       # minus data images
139:       @doc.css("img[src^='data']") -
140:       # minus emojis
141:       @doc.css("img.emoji")
142:   end
143: 
144:   def limit_size!(img)
145:     # retrieve the size from
146:     #  1) the width/height attributes
147:     #  2) the dimension from the preview (image_sizes)
148:     #  3) the dimension of the original image (HTTP request)
149:     w, h =
150:       get_size_from_attributes(img) || get_size_from_image_sizes(img["src"], @opts[:image_sizes]) ||
151:         get_size(img["src"])
152: 
153:     # limit the size of the thumbnail
154:     img["width"], img["height"] = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
155:   end
156: 
157:   def get_size_from_attributes(img)
158:     w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i
159:     return w, h if w > 0 && h > 0
160:     # if only width or height are specified attempt to scale image
161:     if w > 0 || h > 0
162:       w = w.to_f
163:       h = h.to_f
164: 
165:       return unless original_image_size = get_size(img["src"])
166:       original_width, original_height = original_image_size.map(&:to_f)
167: 
168:       if w > 0
169:         ratio = w / original_width
170:         [w.floor, (original_height * ratio).floor]
171:       else
172:         ratio = h / original_height
173:         [(original_width * ratio).floor, h.floor]
174:       end
175:     end
176:   end
177:

180:     image_sizes.each do |image_size|

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module PrettyText
4:   module Helpers
5:     extend self
6: 
7:     TAG_HASHTAG_POSTFIX = "::tag"
8: 
9:     # functions here are available to v8
10:     def t(key, opts)
11:       key = "js." + key
12:       return I18n.t(key) if opts.blank?
13:       str = I18n.t(key, Hash[opts.entries].symbolize_keys).dup
14:       opts.each { |k, v| str.gsub!("{{#{k}}}", v.to_s) }
15:       str
16:     end
17: 
18:     def avatar_template(username)
19:       return "" unless username
20:       user = User.find_by(username_lower: username.downcase)
21:       return "" if user.blank?
22: 
23:       # TODO: Add support for ES6 and call `avatar-template` directly
24:       UrlHelper.schemaless(UrlHelper.absolute(user.avatar_template))
25:     end
26: 
27:     def lookup_primary_user_group(username)
28:       return "" unless username
29:       user = User.find_by(username_lower: username.downcase)
30:       return "" if user.blank?
31: 
32:       user.primary_group.try(:name) || ""
33:     end
34: 
35:     # Overwrite this in a plugin to change how markdown can format
36:     # usernames on the server side
37:     def format_username(username)
38:       username
39:     end
40: 
41:     def lookup_upload_urls(urls)
42:       map = {}
43:       result = {}
44: 
45:       urls.each do |url|
46:         sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_url(url)
47:         if (url.split(".")[1].nil?) # video sha1 without extension for thumbnail
48:           thumbnail = Upload.where("original_filename LIKE ?", "#{sha1}.%").last if sha1
49:           # Fallback for old posts that don't contain data-video-base62-sha1
50:           thumbnail = Upload.where("original_filename LIKE ?", "#{url}.%").last if thumbnail.nil? &&
51:             sha1.nil?
52:           sha1 = thumbnail.sha1 if thumbnail
53:         end
54:         map[url] = sha1 if sha1
55:       end
56: 
57:       if map.length > 0
58:         reverse_map = {}
59: 
60:         map.each do |key, value|
61:           reverse_map[value] ||= []
62:           reverse_map[value] << key
63:         end
64: 
65:         Upload
66:           .where(sha1: map.values)
67:           .pluck(:sha1, :url, :extension, :secure)
68:           .each do |row|
69:             sha1, url, extension, secure = row
70: 
71:             if short_urls = reverse_map[sha1]
72:               secure_uploads = SiteSetting.secure_uploads? && secure
73: 
74:               short_urls.each do |short_url|
75:                 result[short_url] = {
76:                   url:
77:                     (
78:                       if secure_uploads
79:                         Upload.secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url(url)
80:                       else
81:                         Discourse.store.cdn_url(url)
82:                       end
83:                     ),
84:                   short_path: Upload.short_path(sha1: sha1, extension: extension),
85:                   base62_sha1: Upload.base62_sha1(sha1),
86:                 }
87:               end
88:             end
89:           end
90:       end
91: 
92:       result
93:     end
94: 
95:     def get_topic_info(topic_id)
96:       return unless topic_id.is_a?(Integer)
97:       # TODO this only handles public topics, secured one do not get this
98:       topic = Topic.find_by(id: topic_id)
99:       if topic && Guardian.new.can_see?(topic)
100:         { title: Rack::Utils.escape_html(topic.title), href: topic.url }
101:       elsif topic
102:         { title: I18n.t("on_another_topic"), href: topic.slugless_url }
103:       end
104:     end
105: 
106:     def hashtag_lookup(slug, cooking_user_id, types_in_priority_order)
107:       # NOTE: This is _somewhat_ expected since we need to be able to cook posts
108:       # etc. without a user sometimes, but it is still an edge case.
109:       #
110:       # The Discourse.system_user is usually an admin with access to _all_
111:       # categories, however if the suppress_secured_categories_from_admin
112:       # site setting is activated then this user will not be able to access
113:       # secure categories, so hashtags that are secure will not render.
114:       cooking_user = User.find_by(id: cooking_user_id) || Discourse.system_user
115: 
116:       types_in_priority_order =
117:         types_in_priority_order.select do |type|
118:           HashtagAutocompleteService.data_source_types.include?(type)
119:         end
120: 
121:       result =
122:         HashtagAutocompleteService.new(Guardian.new(cooking_user)).lookup(
123:           [slug],
124:           types_in_priority_order,
125:         )
126: 
127:       found_hashtag = nil
128:       types_in_priority_order.each do |type|
129:         if result[type.to_sym].any?
130:           found_hashtag = result[type.to_sym].first.to_h
131:           break
132:         end
133:       end
134:       found_hashtag
135:     end
136: 
137:     def get_current_user(user_id)
138:       return unless user_id.is_a?(Integer)
139:       { staff: User.where(id: user_id).where("moderator OR admin").exists? }
140:     end
141:   end
142: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class UploadRecovery
4:   def initialize(dry_run: false, stop_on_error: false)
5:     @dry_run = dry_run
6:     @stop_on_error = stop_on_error
7:   end
8: 
9:   def recover(posts = Post)
10:     posts.have_uploads.find_each { |post| recover_post post }
11:   end
12: 
13:   def recover_post(post)
14:     begin
15:       analyzer = PostAnalyzer.new(post.raw, post.topic_id)
16: 
17:       analyzer
18:         .cooked_stripped
19:         .css("img", "a")
20:         .each do |media|
21:           if media.name == "img" && orig_src = media["data-orig-src"]
22:             if dom_class = media["class"]
23:               next if (Post.allowed_image_classes & dom_class.split).count > 0
24:             end
25: 
26:             if @dry_run
27:               puts "#{post.full_url} #{orig_src}"
28:             else
29:               recover_post_upload(post, Upload.sha1_from_short_url(orig_src))
30:             end
31:           elsif url = (media["href"] || media["src"])
32:             data = Upload.extract_url(url)
33:             next unless data
34: 
35:             upload = Upload.get_from_url(url)
36: 
37:             if !upload || upload.verification_status == Upload.verification_statuses[:invalid_etag]
38:               if @dry_run
39:                 puts "#{post.full_url} #{url}"
40:               else
41:                 sha1 = data[2]
42:                 recover_post_upload(post, sha1)
43:               end
44:             end
45:           end
46:         end
47:     rescue => e
48:       raise e if @stop_on_error
49:       puts "#{post.full_url} #{e.class}: #{e.message}"
50:     end
51:   end
52: 
53:   private
54: 
55:   def recover_post_upload(post, sha1)
56:     return unless valid_sha1?(sha1)
57: 
58:     attributes = { post: post, sha1: sha1 }
59: 
60:     if Discourse.store.external?
61:       recover_post_upload_from_s3(**attributes)
62:     else
63:       recover_post_upload_from_local(**attributes)
64:     end
65:   end
66: 
67:   def ensure_upload!(post:, sha1:, upload:)
68:     return if !upload.persisted?
69: 
70:     if upload.sha1 != sha1
71:       STDERR.puts "Warning #{post.url} had an incorrect #{sha1} should be #{upload.sha1} storing in custom field 'rake uploads:fix_relative_upload_links' can fix this"
72: 
73:       sha_map = post.custom_fields["UPLOAD_SHA1_MAP"] || "{}"
74:       sha_map = JSON.parse(sha_map)
75:       sha_map[sha1] = upload.sha1
76: 
77:       post.custom_fields["UPLOAD_SHA1_MAP"] = sha_map.to_json
78:       post.save_custom_fields
79:     end
80: 
81:     post.rebake!
82:   end
83: 
84:   def recover_post_upload_from_local(post:, sha1:)
85:     recover_from_local(sha1: sha1, user_id: post.user_id) do |upload|
86:       ensure_upload!(post: post, sha1: sha1, upload: upload)
87:     end
88:   end
89: 
90:   def recover_post_upload_from_s3(post:, sha1:)
91:     recover_from_s3(sha1: sha1, user_id: post.user_id) do |upload|
92:       ensure_upload!(post: post, sha1: sha1, upload: upload)
93:     end
94:   end
95: 
96:   def recover_from_local(sha1:, user_id:)
97:     @paths ||=
98:       begin
99:         Dir.glob(File.join(Discourse.store.tombstone_dir, "original", "**", "*.*")).concat(
100:           Dir.glob(File.join(Discourse.store.upload_path, "original", "**", "*.*")),
101:         )
102:       end
103: 
104:     @paths.each do |path|
105:       if path =~ /#{sha1}/
106:         begin
107:           tmp = Tempfile.new
108:           tmp.write(File.read(path))
109:           tmp.rewind
110: 
111:           upload = create_upload(tmp, File.basename(path), user_id)
112:           yield upload if block_given?
113:         ensure
114:           tmp&.close
115:         end
116:       end
117:     end
118:   end
119: 
120:   def recover_from_s3(sha1:, user_id:)
121:     @object_keys ||=
122:       begin
123:         s3_helper = Discourse.store.s3_helper
124: 
125:         if Rails.configuration.multisite
126:           current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
127:           s3_helper
128:             .list("uploads/#{current_db}/original")
129:             .map(&:key)
130:             .concat(
131:               s3_helper.list(
132:                 "uploads/#{FileStore::S3Store::TOMBSTONE_PREFIX}#{current_db}/original",
133:               ).map(&:key),
134:             )
135:         else
136:           s3_helper
137:             .list("original")
138:             .map(&:key)
139:             .concat(s3_helper.list("#{FileStore::S3Store::TOMBSTONE_PREFIX}original").map(&:key))
140:         end
141:       end
142: 
143:     upload_exists = Upload.exists?(sha1: sha1)
144: 
145:     @object_keys.each do |key|
146:       if key =~ /#{sha1}/
147:         tombstone_prefix = FileStore::S3Store::TOMBSTONE_PREFIX
148: 
149:         if key.include?(tombstone_prefix)
150:           old_key = key
151:           key = key.sub(tombstone_prefix, "")
152:           Discourse.store.copy_file(source: old_key, destination: key, secure: false)
153:         end
154: 
155:         next if upload_exists
156: 
157:         url = "https:#{SiteSetting.Upload.absolute_base_url}/#{key}"
158: 
159:         begin
160:           tmp =
161:             FileHelper.download(
162:               url,
163:               max_file_size: SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes,
164:               tmp_file_name: "recover_from_s3",
165:             )
166: 
167:           if tmp
168:             upload = create_upload(tmp, File.basename(key), user_id)
169:             yield upload if block_given?
170:           end
171:         ensure
172:           tmp&.close
173:         end
174:       end
175:     end
176:   end
177: 
178:   def create_upload(file, filename, user_id)
179:     UploadCreator.new(file, filename).create_for(user_id)
180:   end
181: 
182:   def valid_sha1?(sha1)
183:     sha1.present? && sha1.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
184:   end
185: end

(End of file - total 185 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: require "cache"
4: require "open3"
5: require "plugin/instance"
6: require "version"
7: require "version_compatibility"
8: require "git_utils"
9: 
10: module Discourse
11:   DB_POST_MIGRATE_PATH = "db/post_migrate"
12:   MAX_METADATA_FILE_SIZE = 64.kilobytes
13:   LOCALE_PARAM = "tl"
14: 
15:   class Utils
16:     URI_REGEXP = URI.regexp(%w[http https])
17: 
18:     # Usage:
19:     #   Discourse::Utils.execute_command("pwd", chdir: 'mydirectory')
20:     # or with a block
21:     #   Discourse::Utils.execute_command(chdir: 'mydirectory') do |runner|
22:     #     runner.exec("pwd")
23:     #   end
24:     def self.execute_command(*command, **args)
25:       runner = CommandRunner.new(**args)
26: 
27:       if block_given?
28:         if command.present?
29:           raise RuntimeError.new("Cannot pass command and block to execute_command")
30:         end
31:         yield runner
32:       else
33:         runner.exec(*command)
34:       end
35:     end
36: 
37:     def self.pretty_logs(logs)
38:       logs.join("\n")
39:     end
40: 
41:     def self.logs_markdown(logs, user:, filename: "log.txt")
42:       # Reserve 250 characters for the rest of the text
43:       max_logs_length = SiteSetting.max_post_length - 250
44:       pretty_logs = Discourse::Utils.pretty_logs(logs)
45: 
46:       # If logs are short, try to inline them
47:       return <<~TEXT if pretty_logs.size < max_logs_length
48:         ```text
49:         #{pretty_logs}
50:         ```
51:         TEXT
52: 
53:       # Try to create an upload for the logs
54:       upload =
55:         Dir.mktmpdir do |dir|
56:           File.write(File.join(dir, filename), pretty_logs)
57:           zipfile = Compression::Zip.new.compress(dir, filename)
58:           File.open(zipfile) do |file|
59:             UploadCreator.new(
60:               file,
61:               File.basename(zipfile),
62:               type: "backup_logs",
63:               for_export: "true",
64:             ).create_for(user.id)
65:           end
66:         end
67: 
68:       if upload.persisted?
69:         return UploadMarkdown.new(upload).attachment_markdown
70:       else
71:         Rails.logger.warn("Failed to upload the backup logs file: #{upload.errors.full_messages}")
72:       end
73: 
74:       # If logs are long and upload cannot be created, show trimmed logs
75:       <<~TEXT
76:       ```text
77:       ...
78:       #{pretty_logs.last(max_logs_length)}
79:       ```
80:       TEXT
81:     end
82: 
83:     def self.atomic_write_file(destination, contents)
84:       begin
85:         return if File.read(destination) == contents
86:       rescue Errno::ENOENT
87:       end
88: 
89:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.join(Rails.root, "tmp"))
90:       temp_destination = File.join(Rails.root, "tmp", SecureRandom.hex)
91: 
92:       File.open(temp_destination, "w") do |fd|
93:         fd.write(contents)
94:         fd.fsync()
95:       end
96: 
97:       FileUtils.mv(temp_destination, destination)
98: 
99:       nil
100:     end
101: 
102:     def self.atomic_ln_s(source, destination)
103:       begin
104:         return if File.readlink(destination) == source
105:       rescue Errno::ENOENT, Errno::EINVAL
106:       end
107: 
108:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.join(Rails.root, "tmp"))
109:       temp_destination = File.join(Rails.root, "tmp", SecureRandom.hex)
110:       execute_command("ln", "-s", source, temp_destination)
111: 
112:       # Remove existing symlink first to prevent FileUtils.mv from moving
113:       # the temp file inside the symlinked directory instead of replacing it
114:       File.delete(destination) if File.symlink?(destination)
115: 
116:       FileUtils.mv(temp_destination, destination)
117: 
118:       nil
119:     end

403:   def is_svg?(img)
404:     path =
405:       begin
406:         URI(img["src"]).path
407:       rescue URI::Error
408:         nil
409:       end
410: 
411:     File.extname(path) == ".svg" if path

414:   def convert_to_link!(img)
415:     w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i
416:     user_width, user_height =
417:       (w > 0 && h > 0 && [w, h]) || get_size_from_attributes(img) ||
418:         get_size_from_image_sizes(img["src"], @opts[:image_sizes])
419: 
420:     limit_size!(img)
421: 
422:     src = img["src"]
423:     return if src.blank? || is_a_hyperlink?(img)
424: 
425:     upload = Upload.get_from_url(src)
426: 
427:     original_width, original_height = nil
428: 
429:     if (upload.present?)
430:       original_width = upload.width || 0
431:       original_height = upload.height || 0
432:     else
433:       original_width, original_height = (get_size(src) || [0, 0]).map(&:to_i)
434:       if original_width == 0 || original_height == 0
435:         Rails.logger.info "Can't reach '#{src}' to get its dimension."
436:         return
437:       end
438:     end
439: 
440:     if (upload.present? && upload.animated?) || src.match?(GIF_SOURCES_REGEXP)
441:       img.add_class("animated")
442:     end
443: 
444:     generate_thumbnail =
445:       original_width > SiteSetting.max_image_width || original_height > SiteSetting.max_image_height
446: 
447:     user_width, user_height = [original_width, original_height] if user_width.to_i <= 0 &&
448:       user_height.to_i <= 0
449:     width, height = user_width, user_height
450: 
451:     crop =
452:       SiteSetting.min_ratio_to_crop > 0 && width.to_f / height.to_f < SiteSetting.min_ratio_to_crop
453: 
454:     if crop
455:       width, height = ImageSizer.crop(width, height)
456:       img["width"], img["height"] = width, height
457:     else
458:       width, height = ImageSizer.resize(width, height)
459:     end
460: 
461:     if upload.present?
462:       if generate_thumbnail
463:         upload.create_thumbnail!(width, height, crop: crop)
464: 
465:         each_responsive_ratio do |ratio|
466:           resized_w = (width * ratio).to_i
467:           resized_h = (height * ratio).to_i
468: 
469:           if upload.width && resized_w <= upload.width
470:             upload.create_thumbnail!(resized_w, resized_h, crop: crop)
471:           end
472:         end
473:       end
474: 
475:       return if upload.animated?
476: 
477:       if img.ancestors(".onebox, .onebox-body").blank? && !img.classes.include?("onebox")
478:         add_lightbox!(img, original_width, original_height, upload, crop)
479:       end
480: 
481:       optimize_image!(img, upload, cropped: crop) if generate_thumbnail
482:     end
483:   end
484: 
485:   def process_hotlinked_image(img)
486:     onebox = img.ancestors(".onebox, .onebox-body").first
487: 
488:     # Skip hotlinked media processing if @post is not available (e.g., for chat messages)
489:     return true if @post.nil?
490: 
491:     @hotlinked_map ||= @post.post_hotlinked_media.preload(:upload).index_by(&:url)
492:     normalized_src =
493:       PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(img["src"] || img[PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR])
494:     info = @hotlinked_map[normalized_src]
495: 
496:     still_an_image = true
497: 
498:     if info&.too_large?
499:       if !onebox || onebox.element_children.size == 1
500:         add_large_image_placeholder!(img)
501:       else
502:         img.remove
503:       end
504: 
505:       still_an_image = false
506:     elsif info&.download_failed?
507:       if !onebox || onebox.element_children.size == 1
508:         add_broken_image_placeholder!(img)
509:       else
510:         img.remove
511:       end
512: 
513:       still_an_image = false
514:     elsif info&.downloaded? && upload = info&.upload
515:       img["src"] = UrlHelper.cook_url(upload.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads)
516:       img["data-dominant-color"] = upload.dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: true).presence
517:       img.delete(PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR)
518:     end
519: 
520:     still_an_image
521:   end
522: 
523:   def optimize_image!(img, upload, cropped: false)
524:     w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i
525:     onebox = img.ancestors(".onebox, .onebox-body").first
526: 
527:     # note: optimize_urls cooks the src further after this
528:     thumbnail = upload.thumbnail(w, h)
529:     if thumbnail && thumbnail.filesize.to_i < upload.filesize
530:       img["src"] = thumbnail.url
531: 
532:       srcset = +""
533: 
534:       # Skip srcset for onebox images. Because onebox thumbnails by default
535:       # are fairly small the width/height of the smallest thumbnail is likely larger
536:       # than what the onebox thumbnail size will be displayed at, so we shouldn't
537:       # need to upscale for retina devices
538:       if !onebox
539:         each_responsive_ratio do |ratio|
540:           resized_w = (w * ratio).to_i
541:           resized_h = (h * ratio).to_i
542: 
543:           if !cropped && upload.width && resized_w > upload.width
544:             cooked_url = UrlHelper.cook_url(upload.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads)
545:             srcset << ", #{cooked_url} #{ratio.to_s.sub(/\.0\z/, "")}x"
546:           elsif t = upload.thumbnail(resized_w, resized_h)
547:             cooked_url = UrlHelper.cook_url(t.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads)
548:             srcset << ", #{cooked_url} #{ratio.to_s.sub(/\.0\z/, "")}x"
549:           end
550: 
551:           img[
552:             "srcset"
553:           ] = "#{UrlHelper.cook_url(img["src"], secure: @should_secure_uploads)}#{srcset}" if srcset.present?
554:         end
555:       end
556:     else
557:       img["src"] = upload.url
558:     end
559: 
560:     if !@disable_dominant_color &&
561:          (color = upload.dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: true).presence)
562:       img["data-dominant-color"] = color
563:     end
564:   end
565: 
566:   def add_lightbox!(img, original_width, original_height, upload, crop)
567:     return if original_width < MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH || original_height < MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT
568: 
569:     # first, create a div to hold our lightbox
570:     lightbox = create_node("div", LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS)
571:     img.add_next_sibling(lightbox)
572:     lightbox.add_child(img)
573: 
574:     # then, the link to our larger image
575:     src_url = Upload.secure_uploads_url?(img["src"]) ? upload&.url || img["src"] : img["src"]
576:     src = UrlHelper.cook_url(src_url, secure: @should_secure_uploads)
577: 
578:     a = create_link_node("lightbox", src)
579:     img.add_next_sibling(a)
580: 
581:     a["data-download-href"] = Discourse.store.download_url(upload) if upload
582: 
583:     a.add_child(img)
584: 
585:     # then, some overlay informations
586:     meta = create_node("div", "meta")
587:     img.add_next_sibling(meta)
588: 
589:     filename = get_filename(upload, img["src"])
590:     informations = +"#{original_width}×#{original_height}"
591:     informations << " #{upload.human_filesize}" if upload
592: 
593:     a["title"] = img["title"] || img["alt"] || filename
594: 
595:     meta.add_child create_icon_node("far-image")
596:     meta.add_child create_span_node("filename", a["title"])
597:     meta.add_child create_span_node("informations", informations)
598:     meta.add_child create_icon_node("discourse-expand")
599:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "aws-sdk-s3"
4: require "csv"
5: 
6: class S3Inventory
7:   attr_reader :type, :inventory_date, :s3_helper
8: 
9:   CSV_KEY_INDEX = 1
10:   CSV_ETAG_INDEX = 2
11:   INVENTORY_PREFIX = "inventory"
12:   INVENTORY_LAG = 2.days
13:   WAIT_AFTER_RESTORE_DAYS = 2
14: 
15:   def initialize(
16:     type,
17:     s3_inventory_bucket:,
18:     preloaded_inventory_file: nil,
19:     preloaded_inventory_date: nil,
20:     s3_options: {}
21:   )
22:     @s3_helper = S3Helper.new(s3_inventory_bucket, "", s3_options)
23: 
24:     if preloaded_inventory_file && preloaded_inventory_date
25:       # Data preloaded, so we don't need to fetch it again
26:       @preloaded_inventory_file = preloaded_inventory_file
27:       @inventory_date = preloaded_inventory_date
28:     end
29: 
30:     if type == :upload
31:       @type = "original"
32:       @model = Upload
33:       @scope = @model.by_users.without_s3_file_missing_confirmed_verification_status
34:     elsif type == :optimized
35:       @type = "optimized"
36:       @scope = @model = OptimizedImage
37:     else
38:       raise "Invalid type: #{type}"
39:     end
40:   end
41: 
42:   def backfill_etags_and_list_missing
43:     if !@preloaded_inventory_file && files.blank?
44:       error("Failed to list inventory from S3")
45:       return
46:     end
47: 
48:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("s3_inventory_list_missing_#{type}", validity: 30.minutes) do
49:       begin
50:         download_and_decompress_files if !@preloaded_inventory_file
51: 
52:         multisite_prefix = Discourse.store.upload_path
53: 
54:         ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
55:           begin
56:             connection.exec(
57:               "CREATE TEMP TABLE #{tmp_table_name}(url text UNIQUE, etag text, PRIMARY KEY(etag, url))",
58:             )
59: 
60:             connection.copy_data("COPY #{tmp_table_name} FROM STDIN CSV") do
61:               for_each_inventory_row do |row|
62:                 key = row[CSV_KEY_INDEX]
63: 
64:                 next if Rails.configuration.multisite && key.exclude?(multisite_prefix)
65:                 next if key.exclude?("#{type}/")
66: 
67:                 url = File.join(Discourse.store.absolute_base_url, key)
68:                 connection.put_copy_data("#{CGI.unescape(url)},#{row[CSV_ETAG_INDEX]}\n")
69:               end
70:             end
71: 
72:             table_name = @model.table_name
73: 
74:             # backfilling etags
75:             connection.async_exec(
76:               "UPDATE #{table_name}
77:               SET etag = #{tmp_table_name}.etag
78:               FROM #{tmp_table_name}
79:               WHERE #{table_name}.etag IS NULL AND
80:                 #{table_name}.url = #{tmp_table_name}.url",
81:             )
82: 
83:             uploads = @scope.where("updated_at < ?", inventory_date)
84: 
85:             missing_uploads =
86:               uploads.joins(
87:                 "LEFT JOIN #{tmp_table_name} ON #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag",
88:               ).where("#{tmp_table_name}.etag IS NULL")
89: 
90:             exists_with_different_etag =
91:               missing_uploads
92:                 .joins(
93:                   "LEFT JOIN #{tmp_table_name} inventory2 ON inventory2.url = #{table_name}.url",
94:                 )
95:                 .where.not(inventory2: { etag: nil })
96:                 .pluck(:id)
97: 
98:             # marking as verified/not verified
99:             if @model == Upload
100:               sql_params = {
101:                 inventory_date: inventory_date,
102:                 invalid_etag: Upload.verification_statuses[:invalid_etag],
103:                 s3_file_missing_confirmed: Upload.verification_statuses[:s3_file_missing_confirmed],
104:                 verified: Upload.verification_statuses[:verified],
105:                 seeded_id_threshold: @model::SEEDED_ID_THRESHOLD,
106:               }
107: 
108:               DB.exec(<<~SQL, sql_params)
109:                 UPDATE #{table_name}
110:                 SET verification_status = :verified
111:                 WHERE etag IS NOT NULL
112:                   AND verification_status <> :verified
113:                   AND verification_status <> :s3_file_missing_confirmed
114:                   AND updated_at < :inventory_date
115:                   AND id > :seeded_id_threshold
116:                   AND EXISTS
117:                     (
118:                         SELECT 1
119:                         FROM #{tmp_table_name}
120:                         WHERE #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag
121:                     )
122:               SQL
123: 
124:               DB.exec(<<~SQL, sql_params)
125:                 UPDATE #{table_name}
126:                 SET verification_status = :invalid_etag
127:                 WHERE verification_status <> :invalid_etag
128:                   AND verification_status <> :s3_file_missing_confirmed
129:                   AND updated_at < :inventory_date
130:                   AND id > :seeded_id_threshold
131:                   AND NOT EXISTS
132:                     (
133:                         SELECT 1
134:                         FROM #{tmp_table_name}
135:                         WHERE #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag
136:                     )
137:               SQL
138:             end
139: 
140:             if (missing_count = missing_uploads.count) > 0
141:               missing_uploads
142:                 .select(:id, :url)
143:                 .find_each do |upload|
144:                   if exists_with_different_etag.include?(upload.id)
145:                     log "#{upload.url} has different etag"
146:                   else
147:                     log upload.url
148:                   end
149:                 end
150: 
151:               log "#{missing_count} of #{uploads.count} #{@scope.name.underscore.pluralize} are missing"
152:               if exists_with_different_etag.present?
153:                 log "#{exists_with_different_etag.count} of these are caused by differing etags"
154:                 log "Null the etag column and re-run for automatic backfill"
155:               end
156:             end
157: 
158:             set_missing_s3_discourse_stats(missing_count)
159:           ensure
160:             connection.exec("DROP TABLE #{tmp_table_name}") unless connection.nil?
161:           end
162:         end
163:       ensure
164:         cleanup!
165:       end
166:     end
167:   end
168: 
169:   def for_each_inventory_row
170:     if @preloaded_inventory_file
171:       CSV.foreach(@preloaded_inventory_file) { |row| yield(row) }
172:     else
173:       files.each { |file| CSV.foreach(file[:filename][0...-3]) { |row| yield(row) } }
174:     end
175:   end
176:

180:     log "Downloading inventory file '#{file[:key]}' to tmp directory..."
181:     failure_message = "Failed to inventory file '#{file[:key]}' to tmp directory."
182: 
183:     @s3_helper.download_file(file[:key], file[:filename], failure_message)
184:   end
185: 
186:   def decompress_inventory_file(file)
187:     log "Decompressing inventory file '#{file[:filename]}', this may take a while..."
188:     Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
189:       "gzip",
190:       "--decompress",
191:       file[:filename],
192:       failure_message: "Failed to decompress inventory file '#{file[:filename]}'.",
193:       chdir: tmp_directory,
194:     )
195:   end
196: 
197:   def prepare_for_all_sites
198:     db_names = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.all_dbs
199:     db_files = {}
200: 
201:     db_names.each { |db| db_files[db] = Tempfile.new("#{db}-inventory.csv") }
202: 
203:     download_and_decompress_files
204:     for_each_inventory_row do |row|
205:       key = row[CSV_KEY_INDEX]
206:       row_db = key.match(%r{uploads/([^/]+)/})&.[](1)
207:       if row_db && file = db_files[row_db]
208:         file.write(row.to_csv)
209:       end
210:     end
211: 
212:     db_names.each { |db| db_files[db].rewind }
213: 
214:     db_files
215:   ensure
216:     cleanup!
217:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "mini_mime"
4: require "file_store/s3_store"
5: 
6: module BackupRestore
7:   class Creator
8:     attr_reader :success, :store
9: 
10:     def initialize(user_id, opts = {})
11:       @user_id = user_id
12:       @client_id = opts[:client_id]
13:       @publish_to_message_bus = opts[:publish_to_message_bus] || false
14:       @with_uploads = opts[:with_uploads] == false ? false : include_uploads?
15:       @filename_override = opts[:filename]
16:       @ticket = opts[:ticket]
17: 
18:       initialize_state
19:     end
20: 
21:     def run
22:       ensure_no_operation_is_running
23:       ensure_we_have_a_user
24: 
25:       log "[STARTED]"
26:       log "'#{@user.username}' has started the backup!"
27: 
28:       mark_backup_as_running
29: 
30:       listen_for_shutdown_signal
31: 
32:       ensure_directory_exists(@tmp_directory)
33:       ensure_directory_exists(@archive_directory)
34: 
35:       update_metadata
36:       dump_public_schema
37: 
38:       log "Finalizing backup..."
39: 
40:       @with_uploads ? create_archive : move_dump_backup
41:       upload_archive
42: 
43:       after_create_hook
44:     rescue SystemExit
45:       log "Backup process was cancelled!"
46:     rescue Exception => ex
47:       log "EXCEPTION: " + ex.message
48:       log ex.backtrace.join("\n")
49:     else
50:       @success = true
51:       @backup_filename
52:     ensure
53:       delete_old
54:       clean_up
55:       notify_user
56:       log "Finished!"
57:       publish_completion
58:     end
59: 
60:     def delete_prior_to_n_days
61:       return if Rails.env.development?
62:       store.delete_prior_to_n_days
63:     rescue => ex
64:       log "Something went wrong while deleting backups prior to n days....", ex
65:     end
66: 
67:     protected
68: 
69:     def ensure_no_operation_is_running
70:       raise BackupRestore::OperationRunningError if BackupRestore.is_operation_running?
71:     end
72: 
73:     def ensure_we_have_a_user
74:       raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:user_id) unless @user
75:     end
76: 
77:     def get_parameterized_title
78:       SiteSetting.title.parameterize[...64].presence || "discourse"
79:     end
80: 
81:     def initialize_state
82:       @success = false
83:       @user = User.find_by(id: @user_id)
84:       @logs = []
85:       @store = BackupRestore::BackupStore.create
86:       @current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
87:       @timestamp = Time.now.strftime("%Y-%m-%d-%H%M%S")
88:       @tmp_directory = File.join(Rails.root, "tmp", "backups", @current_db, @timestamp)
89:       @dump_filename = File.join(@tmp_directory, BackupRestore::DUMP_FILE)
90:       @archive_directory = BackupRestore::LocalBackupStore.base_directory(db: @current_db)
91:       filename = @filename_override || "#{get_parameterized_title}-#{@timestamp}"
92:       @archive_basename =
93:         File.join(
94:           @archive_directory,
95:           "#{filename}-#{BackupRestore::VERSION_PREFIX}#{BackupRestore.current_version}",
96:         )
97: 
98:       @backup_filename =
99:         if @with_uploads
100:           "#{File.basename(@archive_basename)}.tar.gz"
101:         else
102:           "#{File.basename(@archive_basename)}.sql.gz"
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106:     def listen_for_shutdown_signal
107:       BackupRestore.clear_shutdown_signal!
108: 
109:       Thread.new do
110:         Thread.current.name = "shutdown_wait"
111: 
112:         RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.with_connection(@current_db) do
113:           while BackupRestore.is_operation_running?
114:             exit if BackupRestore.should_shutdown?
115:             sleep 0.1
116:           end
117:         end
118:       end
119:     end
120: 
121:     def mark_backup_as_running
122:       log "Marking backup as running..."
123:       BackupRestore.mark_as_running!
124:     end
125: 
126:     def update_metadata
127:       log "Updating metadata..."
128:       BackupMetadata.delete_all
129:       BackupMetadata.create!(name: "base_url", value: Discourse.base_url)
130:       BackupMetadata.create!(name: "cdn_url", value: Discourse.asset_host)
131:       BackupMetadata.create!(
132:         name: "s3_base_url",
133:         value: SiteSetting.Upload.enable_s3_uploads ? SiteSetting.Upload.s3_base_url : nil,
134:       )
135:       BackupMetadata.create!(
136:         name: "s3_cdn_url",
137:         value: SiteSetting.Upload.enable_s3_uploads ? SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url : nil,
138:       )
139:       BackupMetadata.create!(
140:         name: "db_name",
141:         value: RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db,
142:       )
143:       BackupMetadata.create!(name: "multisite", value: Rails.configuration.multisite)
144:     end
145: 
146:     def dump_public_schema
147:       log "Dumping the public schema of the database..."
148: 
149:       logs = Queue.new
150:       pg_dump_running = true
151: 
152:       Thread.new do
153:         RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.establish_connection(db: @current_db)
154:         while pg_dump_running
155:           message = logs.pop.strip
156:           log(message) if message.present?
157:         end
158:       end
159: 
160:       IO.popen("#{pg_dump_command} 2>&1") do |pipe|
161:         begin
162:           while line = pipe.readline
163:             logs << line
164:           end
165:         rescue EOFError
166:           # finished reading...
167:         ensure
168:           pg_dump_running = false
169:           logs << ""
170:         end
171:       end
172: 
173:       raise "pg_dump failed" unless $?.success?
174:     end
175: 
176:     def pg_dump_command
177:       db_conf = BackupRestore.database_configuration
178: 
179:       password_argument = "PGPASSWORD='#{db_conf.password}'" if db_conf.password.present?
180:       host_argument = "--host=#{db_conf.host}" if db_conf.host.present?
181:       port_argument = "--port=#{db_conf.port}" if db_conf.port.present?
182:       username_argument = "--username=#{db_conf.username}" if db_conf.username.present?
183: 
184:       [
185:         password_argument, # pass the password to pg_dump (if any)
186:         "pg_dump", # the pg_dump command
187:         "--schema=public", # only public schema
188:         "-T public.pg_*", # exclude tables and views whose name starts with "pg_"
189:         "--file='#{@dump_filename}'", # output to the dump.sql file
190:         "--no-owner", # do not output commands to set ownership of objects
191:         "--no-privileges", # prevent dumping of access privileges
192:         "--verbose", # specifies verbose mode
193:         "--compress=4", # Compression level of 4
194:         host_argument, # the hostname to connect to (if any)
195:         port_argument, # the port to connect to (if any)
196:         username_argument, # the username to connect as (if any)
197:         db_conf.database, # the name of the database to dump
198:       ].join(" ")
199:     end
200:

21: def file_exists?(path)
22:   File.exist?(path) && File.size(path) > 0
23: rescue StandardError
24:   false
25: end
26: 
27: def gather_uploads
28:   public_directory = "#{Rails.root}/public"
29:   current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
30: 
31:   puts "", "Gathering uploads for '#{current_db}'...", ""
32: 
33:   Upload
34:     .where("url ~ '^\/uploads\/'")
35:     .where("url !~ ?", "^\/uploads\/#{current_db}")
36:     .find_each do |upload|
37:       begin
38:         old_db = upload.url[%r{\A/uploads/([^/]+)/}, 1]
39:         from = upload.url.dup
40:         to = upload.url.sub("/uploads/#{old_db}/", "/uploads/#{current_db}/")
41:         source = "#{public_directory}#{from}"
42:         destination = "#{public_directory}#{to}"
43: 
44:         # create destination directory & copy file unless it already exists
45:         unless file_exists?(destination)
46:           `mkdir -p '#{File.dirname(destination)}'`
47:           `cp --link '#{source}' '#{destination}'`
48:         end
49: 
50:         # ensure file has been successfully copied over
51:         raise unless file_exists?(destination)
52: 
53:         # remap links in db
54:         DbHelper.remap(from, to)
55:       rescue StandardError
56:         putc "!"
57:       else
58:         putc "."
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:   puts "", "Done!"
63: end

527: def sync_access_control(async: true, concurrency: 10)
528:   if async
529:     puts "CAUTION: This task may take a long time to complete! There are #{Upload.count} uploads to sync access control metadata for."
530:     puts ""
531:     puts "-" * 30
532:     puts "Syncing access control metadata will be done in Sidekiq jobs in batches of 100 at a time, check Sidekiq queues for `Jobs::SyncAccessControlForUploads` for progress."
533:     Upload
534:       .select(:id)
535:       .find_in_batches(batch_size: 100) { |uploads| adjust_access_controls(uploads.map(&:id)) }
536:     puts "", "Syncing access control metadata complete!"
537:   else
538:     executor = Concurrent::ThreadPoolExecutor.new(min_threads: 1, max_threads: concurrency)
539:     errors = []
540:     current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
541: 
542:     Upload.find_in_batches(batch_size: 100) do |uploads|
543:       uploads
544:         .map do |upload|
545:           Concurrent::Future.execute(executor:) do
546:             RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.with_connection(current_db) do
547:               begin
548:                 Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(upload, remove_existing_acl: true)
549:               rescue => error
550:                 errors << "Error updating access control for upload #{upload.url}: #{error.message}"
551:               end
552:             end
553:           end
554:         end
555:         .each(&:wait)
556:     end
557: 
558:     executor.shutdown
559:     executor.wait_for_termination
560: 
561:     if errors.present?
562:       errors.each { |error| puts error }
563:       exit 1
564:     end
565: 
566:     true
567:   end
568: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   class CleanUpUploads < ::Jobs::Scheduled
5:     every 1.hour
6: 
7:     def execute(args)
8:       grace_period = [SiteSetting.clean_orphan_uploads_grace_period_hours, 1].max
9: 
10:       # Always remove invalid upload records regardless of clean_up_uploads setting.
11:       Upload
12:         .by_users
13:         .where(
14:           "retain_hours IS NULL OR created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * retain_hours",
15:         )
16:         .where("created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
17:         .where(url: "")
18:         .find_each(&:destroy!)
19: 
20:       return unless SiteSetting.clean_up_uploads?
21: 
22:       # Do nothing if the last cleanup was run too recently.
23:       last_cleanup_timestamp = last_cleanup
24:       if last_cleanup_timestamp.present? &&
25:            (Time.zone.now.to_i - last_cleanup_timestamp) < (grace_period / 2).hours
26:         return
27:       end
28: 
29:       result = Upload.by_users
30:       Upload.unused_callbacks&.each { |handler| result = handler.call(result) }
31: 
32:       # 1. Exclude uploads that have retain_hours set and are still within that retention period.
33:       # 2. Exclude uploads created in the grace period.
34:       # 3. Exclude secure uploads that have an access_control_post_id.
35:       # 4. Exclude uploads that are link to an upload reference.
36:       # 5. Exclude uploads that are linked to anything but a Post via UploadReference.
37:       result =
38:         result
39:           .where(
40:             "uploads.retain_hours IS NULL OR uploads.created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * uploads.retain_hours",
41:           )
42:           .where("uploads.created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
43:           .where(
44:             "((uploads.access_control_post_id IS NULL) OR (uploads.access_control_post_id IS NOT NULL AND NOT uploads.secure))",
45:           )
46:           .joins("LEFT JOIN upload_references ON upload_references.upload_id = uploads.id")
47:           .where("upload_references.upload_id IS NULL")
48:           .with_no_non_post_relations
49: 
50:       result.find_each do |upload|
51:         next if Upload.in_use_callbacks&.any? { |callback| callback.call(upload) }
52:         upload.sha1.present? ? upload.destroy : upload.delete
53:       end
54: 
55:       ExternalUploadStub.cleanup!
56: 
57:       self.last_cleanup = Time.zone.now.to_i
58:     end
59: 
60:     def last_cleanup=(timestamp)
61:       Discourse.redis.setex(last_cleanup_key, 7.days.to_i, timestamp.to_s)
62:     end
63: 
64:     def last_cleanup
65:       timestamp = Discourse.redis.get(last_cleanup_key)
66:       timestamp ? timestamp.to_i : timestamp
67:     end
68: 
69:     def reset_last_cleanup!
70:       Discourse.redis.del(last_cleanup_key)
71:     end
72: 
73:     protected
74: 
75:     def last_cleanup_key
76:       "LAST_UPLOAD_CLEANUP"
77:     end
78:   end
79: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   # Sometimes we need to update the access control metadata for a _lot_ of objects on S3 (such as when secure uploads
5:   # is enabled), this is best spread out over many jobs instead of having to do the whole thing serially.
6:   class SyncAccessControlForUploads < ::Jobs::Base
7:     sidekiq_options queue: "low"
8: 
9:     def execute(args)
10:       return if !Discourse.store.external?
11:       return if !args.key?(:upload_ids)
12: 
13:       Upload
14:         .includes(:optimized_images)
15:         .where(id: args[:upload_ids])
16:         .find_in_batches do |uploads|
17:           uploads.each do |upload|
18:             begin
19:               Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(upload, remove_existing_acl: true)
20:             rescue => err
21:               Discourse.warn_exception(
22:                 err,
23:                 message: "Failed to update upload access control",
24:                 env: {
25:                   upload_id: upload.id,
26:                   filename: upload.original_filename,
27:                 },
28:               )
29:             end
30:           end
31:         end
32:     end
33:   end
34: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   class MigrateUploadScheme < ::Jobs::Scheduled
5:     every 10.minutes
6:     sidekiq_options retry: false
7: 
8:     def execute(args)
9:       return unless SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme
10: 
11:       # clean up failed uploads
12:       Upload
13:         .where("created_at < ?", 1.hour.ago)
14:         .where("LENGTH(COALESCE(url, '')) = 0")
15:         .find_each { |upload| upload.destroy! }
16: 
17:       # migrate uploads to new scheme
18:       problems = Upload.migrate_to_new_scheme(limit: 50)
19: 
20:       problems.each do |hash|
21:         upload_id = hash[:upload].id
22:         Discourse.handle_job_exception(
23:           hash[:ex],
24:           error_context(args, "Migrating upload id #{upload_id}", upload_id: upload_id),
25:         )
26:       end
27: 
28:       # clean up failed optimized images
29:       OptimizedImage
30:         .where("LENGTH(COALESCE(url, '')) = 0")
31:         .find_each { |optimized_image| optimized_image.destroy! }
32: 
33:       # Clean up orphan optimized images
34:       OptimizedImage
35:         .joins("LEFT JOIN uploads ON optimized_images.upload_id = uploads.id")
36:         .where("uploads.id IS NULL")
37:         .find_each { |optimized_image| optimized_image.destroy! }
38: 
39:       # Clean up optimized images that needs to be regenerated
40:       OptimizedImage
41:         .joins(:upload)
42:         .where("optimized_images.url NOT LIKE '%/optimized/_X/%'")
43:         .where("uploads.url LIKE '%/original/_X/%'")
44:         .limit(50)
45:         .find_each do |optimized_image|
46:           upload = optimized_image.upload
47:           optimized_image.destroy!
48:           upload.rebake_posts_on_old_scheme
49:         end
50:     end
51:   end
52: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: module Jobs
3:   class ConvertVideo < ::Jobs::Base
4:     sidekiq_options queue: "low", concurrency: 5
5:     MAX_RETRIES = 5
6:     RETRY_DELAY = 30.seconds
7: 
8:     def execute(args)
9:       return if args[:upload_id].blank?
10: 
11:       upload = Upload.find_by(id: args[:upload_id])
12:       return if upload.blank?
13: 
14:       return if OptimizedVideo.exists?(upload_id: upload.id)
15: 
16:       if upload.url.blank?
17:         retry_count = args[:retry_count].to_i
18:         if retry_count < MAX_RETRIES
19:           Jobs.enqueue_in(RETRY_DELAY, :convert_video, args.merge(retry_count: retry_count + 1))
20:           return
21:         else
22:           Rails.logger.error(
23:             "Upload #{upload.id} URL remained blank after #{MAX_RETRIES} retries when optimizing video",
24:           )
25:           return
26:         end
27:       end
28: 
29:       adapter = VideoConversion::AdapterFactory.get_adapter(upload)
30: 
31:       Rails.logger.error("Video conversion failed for upload #{upload.id}") if !adapter.convert
32:     end
33:   end
34: end

121:     end

123:     class ImageBrokenError < StandardError
124:     end

126:     class UploadCreateError < StandardError
127:     end
128: 
129:     def attempt_download(src, user_id)
130:       # secure-uploads endpoint prevents anonymous downloads, so we
131:       # need the presigned S3 URL here
132:       if Upload.secure_uploads_url?(src)
133:         src = Upload.signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url(src, include_content_disposition: false)
134:       end
135: 
136:       hotlinked = download(src)
137:       raise ImageBrokenError if !hotlinked
138:       if File.size(hotlinked.path) > SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes
139:         raise ImageTooLargeError
140:       end
141: 
142:       filename = File.basename(URI.parse(src).path)
143:       filename << File.extname(hotlinked.path) unless filename["."]
144:       upload = UploadCreator.new(hotlinked, filename, origin: src).create_for(user_id)
145: 
146:       if upload.persisted?
147:         upload
148:       else
149:         log(
150:           :info,
151:           "Failed to persist downloaded hotlinked image for post: #{@post_id}: #{src} - #{upload.errors.full_messages.join("\n")}",
152:         )
153:         raise UploadCreateError
154:       end
155:     end
156: 
157:     def extract_images_from(html)
158:       doc = Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(html)
159: 
160:       doc.css("img[src], [#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR}], a.lightbox[href]") -
161:         doc.css("img.avatar") - doc.css(".lightbox img[src]")
162:     end
163: 
164:     def should_download_image?(src, post = nil)
165:       # make sure we actually have a url
166:       return false if src.blank?
167: 
168:       local_bases =
169:         [Discourse.base_url, Discourse.asset_host, SiteSetting.external_emoji_url.presence].compact
170:           .map { |s| normalize_src(s) }
171: 
172:       if Discourse.store.has_been_uploaded?(src) || normalize_src(src).start_with?(*local_bases) ||
173:            src =~ %r{\A/[^/]}i
174:         return false if !(src =~ %r{/uploads/} || Upload.secure_uploads_url?(src))
175: 
176:         # Someone could hotlink a file from a different site on the same CDN,
177:         # so check whether we have it in this database
178:         #
179:         # if the upload already exists and is attached to a different post,
180:         # or the original_sha1 is missing meaning it was created before secure
181:         # media was enabled, then we definitely want to redownload again otherwise
182:         # we end up reusing existing uploads which may be linked to many posts
183:         # already.
184:         upload = Upload.consider_for_reuse(Upload.get_from_url(src), post)
185: 
186:         return !upload.present?
187:       end
188: 
189:       # Don't download non-local images unless site setting enabled
190:       return false unless SiteSetting.download_remote_images_to_local?
191: 
192:       # parse the src
193:       begin
194:         uri = URI.parse(src)
195:       rescue URI::Error
196:         return false
197:       end
198: 
199:       hostname = uri.hostname
200:       return false unless hostname
201: 
202:       # check the domains blocklist
203:       SiteSetting.should_download_images?(src)
204:     end
205: 
206:     def log(log_level, message)
207:       Rails.logger.public_send(
208:         log_level,
209:         "#{RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db}: #{message}",
210:       )
211:     end
212: 
213:     protected
214: 
215:     def replace_encoded_src(src)
216:       PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(src, reset_scheme: false)
217:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   class PullUserProfileHotlinkedImages < ::Jobs::PullHotlinkedImages
5:     def execute(args)
6:       @user_id = args[:user_id]
7:       raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:user_id) if @user_id.blank?
8: 
9:       user_profile = UserProfile.find_by(user_id: @user_id)
10:       return if user_profile.blank? || user_profile.bio_cooked.nil?
11: 
12:       large_image_urls = []
13:       broken_image_urls = []
14:       downloaded_images = {}
15: 
16:       extract_images_from(user_profile.bio_cooked).each do |node|
17:         download_src = original_src = node["src"] || node["href"]
18:         download_src =
19:           "#{SiteSetting.force_https ? "https" : "http"}:#{original_src}" if original_src.start_with?(
20:           "//",
21:         )
22:         normalized_src = normalize_src(download_src)
23: 
24:         next if !should_download_image?(download_src)
25: 
26:         begin
27:           already_attempted_download =
28:             downloaded_images.include?(normalized_src) ||
29:               large_image_urls.include?(normalized_src) ||
30:               broken_image_urls.include?(normalized_src)
31:           if !already_attempted_download
32:             downloaded_images[normalized_src] = attempt_download(download_src, @user_id)
33:           end
34:         rescue ImageTooLargeError
35:           large_image_urls << normalized_src
36:         rescue ImageBrokenError
37:           broken_image_urls << normalized_src
38:         end
39:       rescue => e
40:         raise e if Rails.env.test?
41:         log(
42:           :error,
43:           "Failed to pull hotlinked image (#{download_src}) user: #{@user_id}\n" + e.message +
44:             "\n" + e.backtrace.join("\n"),
45:         )
46:       end
47: 
48:       user_profile.bio_raw =
49:         InlineUploads.replace_hotlinked_image_urls(raw: user_profile.bio_raw) do |match_src|
50:           normalized_match_src = PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(match_src)
51:           downloaded_images[normalized_match_src]
52:         end
53: 
54:       user_profile.skip_pull_hotlinked_image = true
55:       user_profile.save!
56:     end
57:   end
58: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   class BackupChunksMerger < ::Jobs::Base
5:     sidekiq_options queue: "critical", retry: false
6: 
7:     def execute(args)
8:       filename = args[:filename]
9:       identifier = args[:identifier]
10:       chunks = args[:chunks].to_i
11: 
12:       raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:filename) if filename.blank?
13:       raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:identifier) if identifier.blank?
14:       raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:chunks) if chunks <= 0
15: 
16:       backup_path = "#{BackupRestore::LocalBackupStore.base_directory}/#{filename}"
17:       tmp_backup_path = "#{backup_path}.tmp"
18:       # path to tmp directory
19:       tmp_directory =
20:         File.dirname(BackupRestore::LocalBackupStore.chunk_path(identifier, filename, 0))
21: 
22:       # merge all chunks
23:       HandleChunkUpload.merge_chunks(
24:         chunks,
25:         upload_path: backup_path,
26:         tmp_upload_path: tmp_backup_path,
27:         identifier: identifier,
28:         filename: filename,
29:         tmp_directory: tmp_directory,
30:       )
31: 
32:       # push an updated list to the clients
33:       store = BackupRestore::BackupStore.create
34:       data =
35:         ActiveModel::ArraySerializer.new(store.files, each_serializer: BackupFileSerializer).as_json
36:       MessageBus.publish("/admin/backups", data, group_ids: [Group::AUTO_GROUPS[:staff]])
37:     end
38:   end
39: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   class FixOutOfSyncUserUploadedAvatar < ::Jobs::Onceoff
5:     def execute_onceoff(args)
6:       DB.exec(<<~SQL)
7:       WITH X AS (
8:         SELECT
9:           u.id AS user_id,
10:           ua.gravatar_upload_id AS gravatar_upload_id
11:         FROM users u
12:         JOIN user_avatars ua ON ua.user_id = u.id
13:         LEFT JOIN uploads ON uploads.id = u.uploaded_avatar_id
14:         WHERE u.uploaded_avatar_id IS NOT NULL
15:         AND uploads.id IS NULL
16:       )
17:       UPDATE users
18:       SET uploaded_avatar_id = X.gravatar_upload_id
19:       FROM X
20:       WHERE users.id = X.user_id
21:       AND coalesce(uploaded_avatar_id,-1) <> coalesce(X.gravatar_upload_id,-1)
22:       SQL
23:     end
24:   end
25: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class SiteSetting::Update
4:   include Service::Base
5: 
6:   Setting = Struct.new(:name, :value, :backfill, :change)
7: 
8:   options do
9:     attribute :allow_changing_hidden, :array, default: []
10:     attribute :overridden_setting_names, default: {}
11:   end
12: 
13:   policy :current_user_is_admin
14: 
15:   params do
16:     attribute :settings
17: 
18:     before_validation do
19:       dependent_order = SiteSetting.type_supervisor.dependencies.order
20: 
21:       self.settings =
22:         self
23:           .settings
24:           .to_a
25:           .map do |setting|
26:             Setting.new(
27:               setting[:setting_name].to_sym,
28:               setting[:value].to_s.strip,
29:               !!setting[:backfill],
30:               nil,
31:             )
32:           end
33:           .sort_by { |s| dependent_order.index(s.name) }
34:     end
35: 
36:     validates :settings, presence: true
37: 
38:     after_validation do
39:       self.settings =
40:         self.settings.map do |setting|
41:           raw_value = setting.value
42: 
43:           setting.value =
44:             case SiteSetting.type_supervisor.get_type(setting.name)
45:             when :integer
46:               raw_value.tr("^-0-9", "").to_i
47:             when :file_size_restriction
48:               raw_value.tr("^0-9", "").to_i
49:             when :uploaded_image_list
50:               raw_value.blank? ? "" : Upload.get_from_urls(raw_value.split("|")).to_a
51:             when :upload
52:               Upload.get_from_url(raw_value) || ""
53:             else
54:               raw_value
55:             end
56: 
57:           # Make sure to get the correct value here based on type, otherwise
58:           # we can end up inserting a staff action log when e.g. setting the value
59:           # to "false" when the setting value is already false.
60:           setting.value = SiteSetting.type_supervisor.to_rb_value(setting.name, setting.value)
61: 
62:           setting
63:         end
64:     end
65:   end
66: 
67:   policy :settings_are_not_deprecated, class_name: SiteSetting::Policy::SettingsAreNotDeprecated
68:   policy :settings_are_unshadowed_globally,
69:          class_name: SiteSetting::Policy::SettingsAreUnshadowedGlobally
70:   policy :settings_are_visible, class_name: SiteSetting::Policy::SettingsAreVisible
71:   policy :settings_are_configurable, class_name: SiteSetting::Policy::SettingsAreConfigurable
72: 
73:   try do
74:     transaction do
75:       step :save
76:       step :backfill
77:     end
78:   end
79: 
80:   private
81: 
82:   def current_user_is_admin(guardian:)
83:     guardian.is_admin?
84:   end
85: 
86:   def save(params:, options:, guardian:)
87:     params.settings.each do |setting|
88:       detailed_message =
89:         if default_user_preference?(setting)
90:           value =
91:             setting.backfill ? I18n.t("csv_export.boolean_yes") : I18n.t("csv_export.boolean_no")
92:           I18n.t("staff_action_logs.site_setting.update_existing_users", value:)
93:         end
94: 
95:       setting.change =
96:         SiteSetting.set_and_log(
97:           options.overridden_setting_names[setting.name] || setting.name,
98:           setting.value,
99:           guardian.user,
100:           detailed_message,
101:         )
102:     end
103:   end
104: 
105:   def backfill(params:)
106:     params.settings.each do |setting|
107:       next if !setting.backfill || !default_user_preference?(setting)
108: 
109:       SiteSettingUpdateExistingUsers.call(
110:         setting.name.to_s,
111:         setting.change.new_value,
112:         setting.change.previous_value,
113:       )
114:     end
115:   end
116: 
117:   def default_user_preference?(setting)
118:     SiteSetting::DEFAULT_USER_PREFERENCES.include?(setting.name.to_s)
119:   end
120: end
