1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class SiteSetting::SplashScreenImageChanged
4:   include Service::Base
5: 
6:   params { attribute :upload_id, :integer }
7: 
8:   model :upload
9:   model :svg
10:   model :cleaned_svg
11:   step :save_cleaned_svg_upload
12:   step :clear_cache
13: 
14:   private
15: 
16:   def fetch_upload(params:)
17:     Upload.find_by(id: params.upload_id)
18:   end
19: 
20:   def fetch_svg(upload:)
21:     content =
22:       begin
23:         upload.content
24:       rescue StandardError
25:         nil
26:       end
27: 
28:     return nil if content.blank?
29: 
30:     Nokogiri.XML(content).at_css("svg")
31:   end
32: 
33:   def fetch_cleaned_svg(svg:)
34:     # Disables SMIL animations which can cause performance issues,
35:     # CSS animations are preferred.
36:     svg.xpath(
37:       ".//*[local-name()='animate' or local-name()='animateTransform' or local-name()='animateMotion' or local-name()='set']",
38:     ).each(&:remove)
39: 
40:     # Remove explicit dimensions so the SVG scales via viewBox
41:     if svg["viewBox"].present?
42:       svg.remove_attribute("width")
43:       svg.remove_attribute("height")
44:     end
45: 
46:     svg.to_xml
47:   end
48: 
49:   def save_cleaned_svg_upload(cleaned_svg:, upload:)
50:     return if cleaned_svg == upload.content
51: 
52:     Tempfile.open(%w[splash_screen .svg]) do |tmp|
53:       tmp.write(cleaned_svg)
54:       tmp.rewind
55: 
56:       new_sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(tmp.path)
57:       existing = Upload.find_by(sha1: new_sha1)
58: 
59:       if existing && existing.id != upload.id
60:         SiteSetting.splash_screen_image = existing.id
61:       else
62:         old_path = Discourse.store.get_path_for_upload(upload)
63:         old_url = upload.url
64:         upload.tap do |u|
65:           u.sha1 = new_sha1
66:           u.filesize = tmp.size
67:           u.url = Discourse.store.store_upload(tmp, u)
68:           u.save!(validate: false)
69:         end
70: 
71:         Discourse.store.remove_file(old_url, old_path) if upload.url != old_url
72:       end
73:     end
74:   end
75: 
76:   def clear_cache(upload:)
77:     Discourse.cache.delete("splash_screen_svg_#{upload.id}_#{upload.sha1}")
78:   end
79: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "file_helper"
4: 
5: class UploadValidator < ActiveModel::Validator
6:   def validate(upload)
7:     # staff can upload any file in PM
8:     if (upload.for_private_message && SiteSetting.allow_staff_to_upload_any_file_in_pm)
9:       return true if upload.user&.staff?
10:     end
11: 
12:     # check the attachment blocklist
13:     if upload.for_group_message && SiteSetting.allow_all_attachments_for_group_messages
14:       return upload.original_filename =~ SiteSetting.blocked_attachment_filenames_regex
15:     end
16: 
17:     extension = File.extname(upload.original_filename)[1..-1] || ""
18: 
19:     return validate_site_setting_upload(upload, extension) if upload.for_site_setting
20: 
21:     if upload.for_gravatar && FileHelper.supported_gravatar_extensions.include?(extension)
22:       maximum_image_file_size(upload)
23:       return true
24:     end
25: 
26:     return true if changing_upload_security?(upload)
27: 
28:     if is_authorized?(upload, extension)
29:       if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
30:         authorized_image_extension(upload, extension)
31:         maximum_image_file_size(upload)
32:       else
33:         authorized_attachment_extension(upload, extension)
34:         maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
35:       end
36:     end
37:   end
38: 
39:   def validate_site_setting_upload(upload, extension)
40:     unless upload.user&.staff?
41:       upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.unauthorized"))
42:       return false
43:     end
44: 
45:     setting_opts = site_setting_type_hash(upload.site_setting_name)
46:     authorized_extensions = setting_opts[:authorized_extensions]
47: 
48:     if authorized_extensions.present?
49:       unless extension_allowed_for_site_setting?(extension, authorized_extensions)
50:         upload.errors.add(
51:           :original_filename,
52:           I18n.t("upload.unauthorized", authorized_extensions: authorized_extensions),
53:         )
54:         return false
55:       end
56: 
57:       validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
58:     else
59:       unless FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
60:         upload.errors.add(:original_filename, I18n.t("upload.images_only"))
61:         return false
62:       end
63: 
64:       validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
65:     end
66: 
67:     upload.errors.empty?
68:   end
69: 
70:   # this should only be run on existing records, and covers cases of
71:   # upload.update_secure_status being run outside of the creation flow,
72:   # where some cases e.g. have exemptions on the extension enforcement
73:   def changing_upload_security?(upload)
74:     !upload.new_record? &&
75:       upload.changed_attributes.keys.all? do |attribute|
76:         %w[secure security_last_changed_at security_last_changed_reason].include?(attribute)
77:       end
78:   end
79: 
80:   def is_authorized?(upload, extension)
81:     extension_authorized?(upload, extension, authorized_extensions(upload))
82:   end
83: 
84:   def authorized_image_extension(upload, extension)
85:     extension_authorized?(upload, extension, authorized_images(upload))
86:   end
87: 
88:   def maximum_image_file_size(upload)
89:     maximum_file_size(upload, "image")
90:   end
91: 
92:   def authorized_attachment_extension(upload, extension)
93:     extension_authorized?(upload, extension, authorized_attachments(upload))
94:   end
95: 
96:   def maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
97:     maximum_file_size(upload, "attachment")
98:   end
99: 
100:   private
101: 
102:   def site_setting_type_hash(setting_name)
103:     return {} if setting_name.blank?
104:     SiteSetting.type_supervisor.type_hash(setting_name.to_sym)
105:   end
106: 
107:   def validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
108:     return if !upload.validate_file_size
109: 
110:     max_size_kb = setting_opts[:max_file_size_kb]
111: 
112:     if max_size_kb.present?
113:       max_size_bytes = max_size_kb.to_i.kilobytes
114:       if upload.filesize > max_size_bytes
115:         upload.errors.add(
116:           :filesize,
117:           I18n.t(
118:             "upload.attachments.too_large_humanized",
119:             max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(max_size_bytes),
120:           ),
121:         )
122:       end
123:     elsif FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
124:       maximum_image_file_size(upload)
125:     else
126:       maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
127:     end
128:   end
129: 
130:   def extension_allowed_for_site_setting?(extension, authorized_extensions)
131:     extensions_to_set(authorized_extensions).include?(extension.downcase)
132:   end
133: 
134:   def extensions_to_set(exts)
135:     extensions = Set.new
136: 
137:     exts
138:       .gsub(/[\s\.]+/, "")
139:       .downcase
140:       .split("|")
141:       .each { |extension| extensions << extension if extension.exclude?("*") }
142: 
143:     extensions
144:   end
145: 
146:   def authorized_extensions(upload)
147:     extensions =
148:       if upload.for_theme
149:         SiteSetting.theme_authorized_extensions
150:       elsif upload.for_export
151:         SiteSetting.export_authorized_extensions
152:       else
153:         SiteSetting.authorized_extensions
154:       end
155:     extensions_to_set(extensions)
156:   end
157: 
158:   def authorized_images(upload)
159:     authorized_extensions(upload) & FileHelper.supported_images
160:   end
161: 
162:   def authorized_attachments(upload)
163:     authorized_extensions(upload) - FileHelper.supported_images
164:   end
165: 
166:   def authorizes_all_extensions?(upload)
167:     if upload.user&.staff?
168:       return true if SiteSetting.authorized_extensions_for_staff.include?("*")
169:     end
170:     extensions =
171:       if upload.for_theme
172:         SiteSetting.theme_authorized_extensions
173:       elsif upload.for_export
174:         SiteSetting.export_authorized_extensions
175:       else
176:         SiteSetting.authorized_extensions
177:       end
178:     extensions.include?("*")
179:   end
180: 
181:   def extension_authorized?(upload, extension, extensions)
182:     return true if authorizes_all_extensions?(upload)
183: 
184:     staff_extensions = Set.new
185:     if upload.user&.staff?
186:       staff_extensions = extensions_to_set(SiteSetting.authorized_extensions_for_staff)
187:       return true if staff_extensions.include?(extension.downcase)

3: class UploadSettingValidator
4:   def initialize(opts = {})
5:     @opts = opts
6:   end
7: 
8:   def valid_value?(val)
9:     return true if val.blank?
10: 
11:     upload = Upload.find_by(id: val)
12:     upload.present? && additional_validation_passed(upload)
13:   end
14: 
15:   def error_message
16:     return I18n.t("site_settings.errors.invalid_svg") if @opts[:name] == :splash_screen_image
17:     I18n.t("site_settings.errors.invalid_upload")
18:   end
19: 
20:   def additional_validation_passed(upload)
21:     if @opts[:name] == :splash_screen_image
22:       validate_svg(upload)
23:     else
24:       true
25:     end
26:   end
27: 
28:   # We also clean svgs in UploadCreator#clean_svg!,
29:   # but this is a good extra fallback.
30:   def validate_svg(upload)
31:     content =
32:       begin
33:         upload.content
34:       rescue StandardError
35:         nil
36:       end
37:     return false if content.blank?
38: 
39:     svg = Nokogiri.XML(content).at_css("svg")
40:     return false if svg.blank?
41: 
42:     has_scripts = svg.xpath("//*[local-name()='script']").present?
43:     has_event_handlers = svg.xpath("//@*[starts-with(local-name(), 'on')]").present?
44: 
45:     !has_scripts && !has_event_handlers
46:   end
47: end

2: 
3: # Copies the same schema as SiteSetting, since the values and
4: # data types are identical. This table is used as a way for themes
5: # to overrride specific site settings that we make available in
6: # core based on the `themeable` designation on a site setting.
7: #
8: # Creation, updating, and deletion of theme site settings is done
9: # via the `Themes::ThemeSiteSettingManager` service.
10: class ThemeSiteSetting < ActiveRecord::Base
11:   belongs_to :theme
12: 
13:   has_many :upload_references, as: :target, dependent: :destroy
14: 
15:   after_save do
16:     if saved_change_to_value?
17:       if self.data_type == SiteSettings::TypeSupervisor.types[:upload]
18:         UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [self.value], target: self)
19:       elsif self.data_type == SiteSettings::TypeSupervisor.types[:objects] && self.value.present?
20:         upload_ids =
21:           SchemaSettingsObjectValidator.property_values_of_type(
22:             schema: SiteSetting.type_supervisor.type_hash(self.name.to_sym)[:schema],
23:             objects: JSON.parse(self.value),
24:             type: "upload",
25:           )
26: 
27:         UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self) if upload_ids.any?
28:       end
29:     end
30:   end
31: 
32:   # Gets a list of themes that have theme site setting records
33:   # and the associated values for those settings, where the
34:   # value is different from the default site setting value.
35:   #
36:   # @return [Array<Hash>] an array of hashes where each hash contains:
37:   #   - :theme_id [Integer] the ID of the theme
38:   #   - :theme_name [String] the name of the theme
39:   #   - :setting_name [Symbol] the name of the setting
40:   #   - :value [String] the value of the setting
41:   #   - :data_type [Integer] the data type of the setting
42:   def self.themes_with_overridden_settings
43:     sql = <<~SQL
44:       SELECT theme.id AS theme_id, theme.name AS theme_name,
45:         tss.name AS setting_name, tss.value, tss.data_type
46:       FROM themes theme
47:       INNER JOIN theme_site_settings tss ON theme.id = tss.theme_id
48:       WHERE theme.component = false AND tss.name IN (:setting_names)
49:       ORDER BY tss.name, theme.name
50:     SQL
51: 
52:     DB
53:       .query(sql, setting_names: SiteSetting.themeable_site_settings)
54:       .each { |row| row.setting_name = row.setting_name.to_sym }
55:       .select do |row|
56:         # Do not consider this as an "overridden" setting if the value
57:         # is the same as the default site setting value.
58:         row.value !=
59:           SiteSetting
60:             .type_supervisor
61:             .to_db_value(row.setting_name, SiteSetting.defaults[row.setting_name])
62:             .first
63:       end
64:       .each do |row|
65:         row.value =
66:           SiteSetting.type_supervisor.to_rb_value(row.setting_name, row.value, row.data_type)
67:       end
68:   end
69: 
70:   def self.can_access_db?
71:     !GlobalSetting.skip_redis? && !GlobalSetting.skip_db? &&
72:       ActiveRecord::Base.connection.table_exists?(self.table_name)
73:   end
74: 
75:   # Generates a map of theme IDs to their site setting values. When
76:   # there is no theme site setting for a given theme, the default
77:   # site setting value is used.
78:   #
79:   # @return [Hash] a map where keys are theme IDs and values are hashes:
80:   #
81:   # {
82:   #   123 => {
83:   #     setting_name_1 => value_1,
84:   #     setting_name_2 => value_2,
85:   #     ...
86:   #   }
87:   # }
88:   def self.generate_theme_map
89:     # Similar to what SiteSettings::DbProvider and SiteSettings::LocalProcessProvider do
90:     # for their #all method, we can't try to load settings if the DB is not available,
91:     # since this method is called within SiteSetting.refresh! which is called on boot.
92:     return {} if !can_access_db?
93: 
94:     theme_site_setting_values_map = {}
95:     Theme
96:       .includes(:theme_site_settings)
97:       .not_components
98:       .each do |theme|
99:         SiteSetting.themeable_site_settings.each do |setting_name|
100:           setting = theme.theme_site_settings.find { |s| s.name == setting_name.to_s }
101: 
102:           value =
103:             if setting.nil?
104:               SiteSetting.defaults[setting_name]
105:             else
106:               SiteSetting.type_supervisor.to_rb_value(
107:                 setting.name.to_sym,
108:                 setting.value,
109:                 setting.data_type,
110:               )
111:             end
112: 
113:           theme_site_setting_values_map[theme.id] ||= {}
114:           theme_site_setting_values_map[theme.id][setting_name] = value
115:         end
116:       end
117: 
118:     theme_site_setting_values_map
119:   end
120: 
121:   def self.generate_defaults_map
122:     SiteSetting.themeable_site_settings.to_h do |setting_name|
123:       [setting_name, SiteSetting.defaults[setting_name]]
124:     end
125:   end
126: 
127:   def setting_rb_value
128:     SiteSetting.type_supervisor.to_rb_value(self.name, self.value, self.data_type)
129:   end
130: end
131: 
132: # == Schema Information
133: #
134: # Table name: theme_site_settings
135: #
136: #  id         :bigint           not null, primary key
137: #  theme_id   :integer          not null
138: #  name       :string           not null
139: #  data_type  :integer          not null
140: #  value      :text
141: #  created_at :datetime         not null
142: #  updated_at :datetime         not null
143: #
144: # Indexes
145: #
146: #  index_theme_site_settings_on_theme_id           (theme_id)
147: #  index_theme_site_settings_on_theme_id_and_name  (theme_id,name) UNIQUE
148: #

74:     return nil unless original = image_upload
75:     return nil if original.filesize >= SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.to_i.kilobytes
76:     return nil unless original.read_attribute(:width) && original.read_attribute(:height)

80:       max_width: nil,
81:       max_height: nil,
82:       width: original.width,

84:       url: original.url,
85:     }
86:

97:         url: record.optimized_image&.url,
98:       }

106:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # This model indicates an 'attempt' to create a topic thumbnail
4: # for an upload. This means we don't keep trying to create optimized
5: # images for small/invalid original images.
6: #
7: # Foreign keys with ON DELETE CASCADE are used to ensure unneeded data
8: # is deleted automatically
9: class TopicThumbnail < ActiveRecord::Base
10:   belongs_to :upload
11:   belongs_to :optimized_image
12: 
13:   def self.find_or_create_for!(original, max_width:, max_height:)
14:     existing =
15:       TopicThumbnail.find_by(upload: original, max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height)
16:     return existing if existing
17:     return nil if !SiteSetting.create_thumbnails?
18: 
19:     target_width, target_height =
20:       ImageSizer.resize(
21:         original.width,
22:         original.height,
23:         { max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height },
24:       )
25: 
26:     needs_optimization = target_width < original.width && target_height < original.height
27:     skip_animated = SiteSetting.animated_topic_thumbnails && original.animated?
28: 
29:     if needs_optimization && !skip_animated
30:       optimized = OptimizedImage.create_for(original, target_width, target_height)
31:     end
32: 
33:     # may have been associated already, bulk insert will skip dupes
34:     TopicThumbnail.insert_all(
35:       [
36:         upload_id: original.id,
37:         max_width: max_width,
38:         max_height: max_height,
39:         optimized_image_id: optimized&.id,
40:       ],
41:     )
42: 
43:     TopicThumbnail.find_by(upload: original, max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height)
44:   end
45: 
46:   def self.ensure_consistency!
47:     # Clean up records for broken upload links or broken optimized image links
48:     TopicThumbnail
49:       .joins("LEFT JOIN uploads on upload_id = uploads.id")
50:       .joins("LEFT JOIN optimized_images on optimized_image_id = optimized_images.id")
51:       .where(<<~SQL)
52:         (optimized_image_id IS NOT NULL AND optimized_images IS NULL)
53:         OR uploads IS NULL
54:       SQL
55:       .delete_all
56: 
57:     # Delete records for sizes which are no longer needed
58:     sizes =
59:       Topic.thumbnail_sizes +
60:         ThemeModifierHelper.new(theme_ids: Theme.pluck(:id)).topic_thumbnail_sizes
61:     sizes_sql =
62:       sizes.map { |s| "(max_width = #{s[0].to_i} AND max_height = #{s[1].to_i})" }.join(" OR ")
63:     TopicThumbnail.where.not(sizes_sql).delete_all
64:   end
65: end
66:

70: #
71: #  id                 :bigint           not null, primary key
72: #  upload_id          :bigint           not null

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Reports::TopUploads
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   class_methods do
7:     def report_top_uploads(report)
8:       report.modes = [Report::MODES[:table]]
9: 
10:       extension_filter = report.filters.dig(:file_extension)
11:       report.add_filter(
12:         "file_extension",
13:         type: "list",
14:         default: extension_filter || "any",
15:         choices: (SiteSetting.authorized_extensions.split("|") + Array(extension_filter)).uniq,
16:       )
17: 
18:       report.labels = [
19:         {
20:           type: :link,
21:           properties: %i[file_url file_name],
22:           title: I18n.t("reports.top_uploads.labels.filename"),
23:         },
24:         {
25:           type: :user,
26:           properties: {
27:             username: :author_username,
28:             id: :author_id,
29:             avatar: :author_avatar_template,
30:           },
31:           title: I18n.t("reports.top_uploads.labels.author"),
32:         },
33:         {
34:           type: :text,
35:           property: :extension,
36:           title: I18n.t("reports.top_uploads.labels.extension"),
37:         },
38:         { type: :bytes, property: :filesize, title: I18n.t("reports.top_uploads.labels.filesize") },
39:       ]
40: 
41:       report.data = []
42: 
43:       sql = <<~SQL
44:       SELECT
45:       u.id as user_id,
46:       u.username,
47:       u.uploaded_avatar_id,
48:       up.filesize,
49:       up.original_filename,
50:       up.extension,
51:       up.url
52:       FROM uploads up
53:       JOIN users u
54:       ON u.id = up.user_id
55:       /*where*/
56:       ORDER BY up.filesize DESC
57:       LIMIT #{report.limit || 250}
58:       SQL
59: 
60:       builder = DB.build(sql)
61:       builder.where(
62:         "up.id > :seeded_id_threshold",
63:         seeded_id_threshold: Upload::SEEDED_ID_THRESHOLD,
64:       )
65:       builder.where("up.created_at >= :start_date", start_date: report.start_date)
66:       builder.where("up.created_at < :end_date", end_date: report.end_date)
67:       builder.where("username != '#{Discourse.system_user.username}'")
68: 
69:       if extension_filter
70:         builder.where("up.extension = :extension", extension: extension_filter.sub(/\A\./, ""))
71:       end
72: 
73:       builder.query.each do |row|
74:         data = {}
75:         data[:author_id] = row.user_id
76:         data[:author_username] = row.username
77:         data[:author_avatar_template] = User.avatar_template(row.username, row.uploaded_avatar_id)
78:         data[:filesize] = row.filesize
79:         data[:extension] = row.extension
80:         data[:file_url] = Discourse.store.cdn_url(row.url)
81:         data[:file_name] = row.original_filename.truncate(25)
82:         report.data << data
83:       end
84:     end
85:   end
86: end

322:       @mediaconvert_client ||= build_client
323:     end

325:     def build_client
326:       # For some reason the endpoint is not visible in the aws console UI so we need to get it from the API
327:       if SiteSetting.mediaconvert_endpoint.blank?
328:         client = create_basic_client
329:         resp = client.describe_endpoints
330:         SiteSetting.mediaconvert_endpoint = resp.endpoints[0].url
331:       end
332: 
333:       # Validate that we have an endpoint before proceeding
334:       if SiteSetting.mediaconvert_endpoint.blank?
335:         error_msg = "MediaConvert endpoint is required but could not be discovered"

337:           StandardError.new(error_msg),
338:           message: error_msg,
339:           env: {
340:             upload_id: @upload.id,
341:           },
342:         )
343:         raise StandardError, error_msg
344:       end
345: 
346:       create_basic_client(endpoint: SiteSetting.mediaconvert_endpoint)
347:     end
348: 
349:     def create_basic_client(endpoint: nil)
350:       client_options = { region: SiteSetting.s3_region }
351:       client_options[:endpoint] = endpoint if endpoint.present?
352:       creds = S3Helper.s3_credentials(SiteSetting)
353:       client_options[:credentials] = creds if creds
354: 
355:       Aws::MediaConvert::Client.new(client_options)
356:     end

# Upload contract audit

## Step 1 — Orient in the codebase

This is **Discourse**, a Rails/Ember monolith. Uploaded files are represented by the `Upload` ActiveRecord model. The code that creates, stores, secures, renders, and destroys uploads is split across:

- Core model: `app/models/upload.rb`
- Shared concerns: `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb`, `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb`
- Polymorphic join table: `app/models/upload_reference.rb`
- Derived records: `app/models/optimized_image.rb`, `app/models/optimized_video.rb`, `app/models/topic_thumbnail.rb`
- File stores: `lib/file_store/base_store.rb`, `local_store.rb`, `s3_store.rb`
- Creation / security: `lib/upload_creator.rb`, `lib/upload_security.rb`, `lib/topic_upload_security_manager.rb`
- Rendering helpers: `lib/inline_uploads.rb`, `lib/upload_markdown.rb`, `lib/pretty_text/helpers.rb`, `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb`, `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb`
- Controllers / API: `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb`, `app/controllers/users_controller.rb`, `app/controllers/user_avatars_controller.rb`, etc.
- Background jobs: `app/jobs/regular/*upload*.rb`, `app/jobs/scheduled/*upload*.rb`
- Rake tasks: `lib/tasks/uploads.rake`, `lib/tasks/posts.rake`, `lib/tasks/site.rake`

## Step 2 — Map the Upload contract and shared pieces

| File | Line(s) | What it contributes to the contract |
|---|---|---|
| `app/models/upload.rb` | 5 | `Upload` model class |
| | 7 | `include HasUrl` — URL/SHA1 resolution |
| | 17–33 | Associations: `user`, `access_control_post`, `post_hotlinked_media`, `optimized_images`, `optimized_videos`, `optimized_video_uploads`, `user_uploads`, `upload_references`, `posts`, `topic_thumbnails`, `badges` |
| | 35 | `after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job` |
| | 53–64 | `before_destroy` / `after_destroy` cleanup of `UserProfile`/`User`/`UserAvatar` FKs |
| | 66–75 | Scopes `by_users`, verification-status scopes |
| | 86 | `Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)` |
| | 120 | `with_no_non_post_relations` |
| | 131–690 | Instance API: `to_s`, `to_markdown`, `thumbnail`, `create_thumbnail!`, `content`, `fix_image_extension`, `destroy`, `short_url`, `short_path`, `local?`, `fix_dimensions!`, `dominant_color`, `update_secure_status`, etc. |
| `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb` | 7 | `extract_url` |
| | 11 | `extract_sha1` |
| | 21 | `get_from_url` |
| | 49 | `get_from_urls` |
| `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb` | 6 | `link_post_uploads` |
| | 56 | `each_upload_url` |
| `app/models/upload_reference.rb` | 4 | `belongs_to :upload`, polymorphic `target` |
| | 9 | `ensure_exist!` — the central reference-sync primitive |
| `app/models/optimized_image.rb` | 5 | `belongs_to :upload`, includes `HasUrl` |
| | 35 | `OptimizedImage.create_for(upload, width, height)` |
| | 153 | `destroy` removes file via store |
| `app/models/optimized_video.rb` | 4–5 | `belongs_to :upload`, `belongs_to :optimized_upload, class_name: "Upload"` |
| | 12 | `create_for` builds a second `Upload` for the converted video |
| | 47 | `destroy` cascades to file + optimized upload |
| `app/models/topic_thumbnail.rb` | 9 | `belongs_to :upload` |
| | 13 | `find_or_create_for!(original, max_width:, max_height:)` |
| `app/models/user_upload.rb` | 4 | `belongs_to :upload` (who-can-use guard) |
| `app/models/post_hotlinked_media.rb` | 5 | `belongs_to :upload` |
| `lib/upload_security.rb` | 27 | Decides whether an upload must be secure |
| `lib/topic_upload_security_manager.rb` | 18 | Re-evaluates security when topics/categories move |
| `lib/validators/upload_validator.rb` | 5 | Validates extensions, sizes, site-setting rules |

## Step 3 — How a file becomes a stored Upload

| Layer | File:line | What happens | Sync / Async |
|---|---|---|---|
| HTTP entry | `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:25` | `create` action receives file/url | sync (hijacked) |
| | `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:299` | `UploadsController.create_upload` builds temp file/url | sync |
| Creation | `lib/upload_creator.rb:44` / `:59` | `UploadCreator.new(...).create_for(user_id)` | sync |
| | `lib/upload_creator.rb:91` | `DistributedMutex` around `upload_#{user_id}_#{filename}` | sync |
| | `lib/upload_creator.rb:93–113` | HEIF→JPEG, SVG clean, ICO→PNG, crop/optimize/downsize | sync |
| | `lib/upload_creator.rb:121–178` | SHA1 / unique-hash, duplicate check, build `Upload` record | sync |
| Security | `lib/upload_creator.rb:219–224` | `UploadSecurity.new(@upload, creating: true)` | sync |
| Storage | `lib/upload_creator.rb:244–263` | `Discourse.store.store_upload` or `move_existing_stored_upload` | sync |
| Store dispatch | `lib/discourse.rb:958` | `Discourse.store` returns `FileStore::LocalStore` or `S3Store` | sync |
| Local store | `lib/file_store/local_store.rb:7` | `store_file` writes to `public/uploads/<db>/...` | sync |
| S3 store | `lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:32` | `store_upload` uploads to S3 key + sets ACL/tagging | sync |
| Variants | `app/models/optimized_image.rb:35` | `OptimizedImage.create_for` | sync (triggered by post processor) |
| | `app/models/topic_thumbnail.rb:13` | `TopicThumbnail.find_or_create_for!` | sync |
| | `app/jobs/regular/create_avatar_thumbnails.rb:13` | avatar thumbnail job | async |
| Post processing | `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb:34` | `post_process` calls `link_post_uploads`, `update_post_image`, `update_uploads_secure_status` | async (`Jobs::ProcessPost`) |
| | `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:461` | `upload.create_thumbnail!` for large images | sync |
| Video | `app/models/upload.rb:35` | `after_create` enqueues `Jobs::ConvertVideo` | async |
| | `app/jobs/regular/convert_video.rb:11` | finds upload, runs video adapter | async |
| | `app/models/optimized_video.rb:12` | creates a new `Upload` for the converted file | async |
| Remote images | `app/jobs/regular/pull_hotlinked_images.rb:144` | `UploadCreator.new(hotlinked, ...).create_for` | async |
| Cleanup | `app/jobs/scheduled/clean_up_uploads.rb:7` | destroys orphan uploads hourly | scheduled |

## Step 6 — Blast radius of a contract change

### High risk — will break immediately if URL/path/destroy behavior changes

| File | Why it is high risk |
|---|---|
| `lib/upload_creator.rb` | Defines how an `Upload` is born; any change to required columns, security flags, or storage contract is consumed here first. |
| `lib/file_store/base_store.rb`, `local_store.rb`, `s3_store.rb` | Every read/write/delete path for uploads and optimized images goes through these. |
| `app/models/optimized_image.rb`, `optimized_video.rb`, `topic_thumbnail.rb` | Spawn derived records from an upload; destroying or re-keying an upload must cascade correctly. |
| `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb`, `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb` | Post cooking relies on `upload.url`, `upload.width/height`, `upload.thumbnail`, `upload.secure?`, and `Upload.get_from_url`. |
| `app/models/post.rb`, `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb` | `link_post_uploads` builds the reference graph from cooked HTML; URL/SHA1 format changes break linkage. |
| `lib/inline_uploads.rb`, `lib/upload_markdown.rb`, `lib/pretty_text/helpers.rb` | Short URL format (`upload://`) and `Upload.short_path` are hard-coded assumptions. |
| `lib/upload_security.rb`, `lib/topic_upload_security_manager.rb` | `secure?`, `access_control_post_id`, and `update_secure_status` are central to permissions and S3 ACLs. |
| `app/jobs/scheduled/clean_up_uploads.rb` | Teardown logic; changing what makes an upload “orphan” or how `destroy` removes files will break cleanup. |
| `lib/upload_recovery.rb` | Assumes tombstone path layout and `Upload` SHA1 lookup. |
| `lib/backup_restore/uploads_restorer.rb` | Assumes URL format and directory layout for restore. |
| `app/models/user.rb`, `app/models/user_profile.rb`, `app/models/user_avatar.rb` | Avatar/profile background FKs and teardown cleanup. |
| `app/models/site_setting.rb`, `app/models/theme_setting.rb`, `app/models/theme_field.rb` | Site-setting/theme upload references and serialized URLs. |

### Medium risk — needs review and re-verification

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb`, `app/controllers/users_controller.rb`, `app/controllers/user_avatars_controller.rb`, `app/controllers/metadata_controller.rb` | Serving URLs and metadata; any change to `url_for`, `short_path`, or secure-redirect logic needs controller-level tests. |
| `app/serializers/*` | Many expose `upload.url` or upload IDs; serialization shape may need updates. |
| `lib/email/receiver.rb`, `lib/email/styles.rb` | Attachment deduplication and secure-image inlining rely on `sha1`/`original_sha1`. |
| `app/jobs/regular/*` | Most jobs fetch by `upload_id` and call store methods; confirm async behavior after teardown changes. |
| `lib/shrink_uploaded_image.rb` | Rewrites posts and swaps upload records; very sensitive to URL/SHA1 format. |
| `lib/site_icon_manager.rb` | Site icons resolved through `Upload` ids and `OptimizedImage`. |
| `lib/site_setting_extension.rb`, `lib/theme_settings_manager/upload.rb` | Upload-type setting getters return `Upload` objects or URLs. |

### Lower risk — mostly reads IDs or sums filesize

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `app/models/concerns/reports/*.rb`, `app/models/user_action.rb`, `app/models/incoming_links_report.rb` | Read `uploaded_avatar_id` or `uploads` table for reports. |
| `lib/disk_space.rb` | `Upload.sum(:filesize)`. |
| `lib/discourse_dev/user.rb`, `lib/faker/discourse_markdown.rb` | Dev seeding; should keep working if creation API is stable. |
